TCS NQT June 2026 — Complete 30-Day Study Material + Practice + Tracker

A complete 30-day preparation dashboard from 27 May 2026 to 25 June 2026 for the TCS NQT exam scheduled on 26 June 2026.

Exam Date26 June 2026
Duration190 minutes
Total Questions92
Negative MarkingNo
Countdown loading...

How to use this dashboard

  1. Start from the assigned day and read each topic explanation before attempting the practice bank.
  2. Use the tricks card to reduce solving time without losing accuracy.
  3. Attempt at least 65 questions daily, then submit the quiz to update score and weak areas.
  4. Mark topic completion and day completion. Progress is saved automatically in this browser through LocalStorage.
  5. Before the exam, use the final revision section and print-friendly summary.

Weak Area Dashboard

Topics below 60% are weak, 60–80% are average, and above 80% are strong. Submit daily quizzes to populate this dashboard automatically.

Recommended action rule

  • Below 60%: Revise formulas, redo solved examples, and reattempt easy questions first.
  • 60–80%: Practise medium questions and revisit common mistakes.
  • Above 80%: Attempt hard and mixed questions to improve speed.

Day 01 • 27 May 2026

Day 1 — Number System + Verbal Foundation

Study objective: Build practical command over Number System basics, Place value, Even/odd numbers, Prime/composite numbers and apply it in TCS NQT-style timed questions.

Previous DayNext Day
Day mini TOC: Number System basics Place value Even/odd numbers Prime/composite numbers Divisibility rules Unit digit basics Remainder basics Verbal: Reading comprehension basics Verbal: 10 vocabulary words PracticeQuizScoreRevision

Topics covered

  • Number System basics
  • Place value
  • Even/odd numbers
  • Prime/composite numbers
  • Divisibility rules
  • Unit digit basics
  • Remainder basics
  • Verbal: Reading comprehension basics
  • Verbal: 10 vocabulary words
Number System basics

Number System basics is the foundation of quantitative aptitude. Start by understanding what a number represents, how digits contribute through place value, and how divisibility or remainder rules reduce calculation. In TCS NQT, these questions are usually short but accuracy matters. Solve by checking the structure of the number before doing long calculation.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Number System basics.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Number System basics, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Number System basics; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember Last digit trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Number System basics.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Number System basics is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Last digit trick, Unit digit trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: Find whether 729 is divisible by 9.
Digit sum = 7+2+9 = 18. Since 18 is divisible by 9, 729 is divisible by 9.
Shortcut: Digit sum rule avoids division.
Example 2: Find LCM of 12 and 18.
HCF is 6. LCM = 12×18/6 = 36.
Shortcut: Use product/HCF.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Number System basics without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.

Verbal: Reading comprehension basics

Verbal: Reading comprehension basics improves comprehension, grammar, and vocabulary. Read the sentence meaning first, then check grammar rules. In comprehension, answer only from the passage, not from personal knowledge.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Verbal: Reading comprehension basics.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Verbal: Reading comprehension basics, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Verbal: Reading comprehension basics; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember Elimination trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Verbal: Reading comprehension basics.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Verbal: Reading comprehension basics is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Elimination trick, Connector trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: Choose correct: 'He go' or 'He goes'.
'He goes' is correct because singular subject uses singular verb.
Shortcut: Check subject first.
Example 2: Synonym of rapid.
Rapid means fast or quick.
Shortcut: Use context clue.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Verbal: Reading comprehension basics without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.
Verbal: 10 vocabulary words

Verbal: 10 vocabulary words requires pattern recognition. Check differences, double differences, multiplication, division, squares, cubes, alternate terms, and alphabet positions. Do not force only one pattern; test options quickly.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Verbal: 10 vocabulary words.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

The usual trap in Verbal: 10 vocabulary words is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Pattern recognition trick, Alternate term trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: Find next: 3, 7, 11, 15, ?
Difference is +4, so next = 19.
Shortcut: Check difference first.
Example 2: A>B and B>C. Who is tallest?
A is tallest because A>B>C.
Shortcut: Write comparison chain.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Verbal: 10 vocabulary words without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.

Important Formulas and Rules

Formula/RuleExpressionWhen to use
Divisibility by 2Last digit is evenUse for quick even/odd and factor checks.
Divisibility by 3Sum of digits divisible by 3Use before doing long division.
Divisibility by 9Sum of digits divisible by 9Useful for remainders and shortcuts.
Subject-verb agreementSingular subject takes singular verbCheck subject before verb.
Article usagea/an/theUse an before vowel sound.
PrepositionsDepend on verb phraseLearn common pairs.
Arithmetic seriesCheck differenceMost common first check.
Double differenceCheck change in differencesFor quadratic patterns.
Alphabet codingA=1, B=2, ..., Z=26Use positions.

Practice Question Bank — 65 Questions

All questions below are original TCS NQT PYQ-style or concept-practice questions with answers and explanations.

Q1. What is the unit digit of 8^25?

Number System basicsunit digitMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 0
  2. B. 2
  3. C. 8
  4. D. 4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 8

Explanation: Unit digits follow cycles. For base 8, calculate powers modulo 10. Since 25 is reduced within the unit-digit cycle, the unit digit is 8.

Shortcut: Use the unit digit cycle; never expand the full power.

Q2. Find the remainder when (6 × 20 + 1) is divided by 7.

Place valueremainderMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 5
  2. B. 2
  3. C. 4
  4. D. 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 2

Explanation: First calculate 6 × 20 + 1 = 121. Divide by 7. The remainder is 2.

Shortcut: For remainder questions, reduce each term by the divisor before multiplying.

Q3. Using divisibility rules, which statement is true for 95?

Even/odd numbersprime/compositeHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. divisible by 9
  2. B. not divisible by 3
  3. C. multiple of 11
  4. D. prime number
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. not divisible by 3

Explanation: Sum of digits of 95 is 14. A number is divisible by 3 when the digit sum is divisible by 3. Therefore, 95 is not divisible by 3.

Shortcut: For 3 and 9, check digit sum instead of division.

Q4. Classify the number 34.

Prime/composite numbersplace valueEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Even
  2. B. Odd only
  3. C. Co-prime only
  4. D. Composite
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Composite

Explanation: Check divisibility up to √34. Since a divisor is found, 34 is composite.

Shortcut: To test prime, check only divisors up to square root.

Q5. In the number 7402, what is the place value of the digit 4?

Divisibility rulesdivisibilityMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 4
  2. B. 400
  3. C. 4000
  4. D. 40
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 400

Explanation: Place value means digit multiplied by its position value. In 7402, digit 4 is at the 100 place, so place value = 4 × 100 = 400.

Shortcut: Read from right: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands. Multiply the digit by its place.

Q6. What is the unit digit of 13^30?

Unit digit basicsunit digitMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 5
  2. B. 3
  3. C. 1
  4. D. 9
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 9

Explanation: Unit digits follow cycles. For base 13, calculate powers modulo 10. Since 30 is reduced within the unit-digit cycle, the unit digit is 9.

Shortcut: Use the unit digit cycle; never expand the full power.

Q7. Find the remainder when (11 × 25 + 1) is divided by 5.

Remainder basicsremainderHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 3
  2. B. 1
  3. C. 2
  4. D. 4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 1

Explanation: First calculate 11 × 25 + 1 = 276. Divide by 5. The remainder is 1.

Shortcut: For remainder questions, reduce each term by the divisor before multiplying.

Q8. Fill in the blank: ___ it was raining, the team continued the practice.

Verbal: Reading comprehension basicsreading inferenceEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. because
  2. B. although
  3. C. and
  4. D. therefore
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. although

Explanation: 'Although' shows contrast between rain and continuing practice.

Shortcut: Use although/though when the second part is surprising or contrasting.

Q9. Find the odd one out: Apple, Mango, Chair, Banana.

Verbal: 10 vocabulary wordswrong numberMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Chair
  2. B. Mango
  3. C. Apple
  4. D. Banana
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Chair

Explanation: Apple, Mango and Banana are fruits. Chair is furniture. Therefore Chair is the odd one out.

Shortcut: Classify options by category before eliminating.

Q10. In the number 7447, what is the place value of the digit 4?

Number System basicsdivisibilityMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 4
  2. B. 400
  3. C. 4000
  4. D. 40
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 400

Explanation: Place value means digit multiplied by its position value. In 7447, digit 4 is at the 100 place, so place value = 4 × 100 = 400.

Shortcut: Read from right: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands. Multiply the digit by its place.

Q11. What is the unit digit of 10^35?

Place valueunit digitHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 6
  2. B. 0
  3. C. 2
  4. D. 4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 0

Explanation: Unit digits follow cycles. For base 10, calculate powers modulo 10. Since 35 is reduced within the unit-digit cycle, the unit digit is 0.

Shortcut: Use the unit digit cycle; never expand the full power.

Q12. Find the remainder when (7 × 30 + 1) is divided by 10.

Even/odd numbersremainderEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 3
  2. B. 1
  3. C. 2
  4. D. 4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 1

Explanation: First calculate 7 × 30 + 1 = 211. Divide by 10. The remainder is 1.

Shortcut: For remainder questions, reduce each term by the divisor before multiplying.

Q13. Using divisibility rules, which statement is true for 105?

Prime/composite numbersprime/compositeMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. multiple of 11
  2. B. prime number
  3. C. divisible by 9
  4. D. divisible by 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. divisible by 3

Explanation: Sum of digits of 105 is 6. A number is divisible by 3 when the digit sum is divisible by 3. Therefore, 105 is divisible by 3.

Shortcut: For 3 and 9, check digit sum instead of division.

Q14. Classify the number 44.

Divisibility rulesplace valueMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Even
  2. B. Odd only
  3. C. Co-prime only
  4. D. Composite
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Composite

Explanation: Check divisibility up to √44. Since a divisor is found, 44 is composite.

Shortcut: To test prime, check only divisors up to square root.

Q15. In the number 7492, what is the place value of the digit 4?

Unit digit basicsdivisibilityHardConcept Practice
  1. A. 4
  2. B. 400
  3. C. 4000
  4. D. 40
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 400

Explanation: Place value means digit multiplied by its position value. In 7492, digit 4 is at the 100 place, so place value = 4 × 100 = 400.

Shortcut: Read from right: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands. Multiply the digit by its place.

Q16. What is the unit digit of 7^40?

Remainder basicsunit digitEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 7
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 5
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 1

Explanation: Unit digits follow cycles. For base 7, calculate powers modulo 10. Since 40 is reduced within the unit-digit cycle, the unit digit is 1.

Shortcut: Use the unit digit cycle; never expand the full power.

Q17. In reading comprehension, what do we call a conclusion drawn from clues in the passage?

Verbal: Reading comprehension basicssentence fitMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. pronunciation
  2. B. calculation
  3. C. inference
  4. D. spelling
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. inference

Explanation: A conclusion based on clues and logic from the passage is called an inference.

Shortcut: Inference = information implied, not directly stated.

Q18. In analogy 'Pen : Write', identify the relationship.

Verbal: 10 vocabulary wordsmissing numberMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Number pattern
  2. B. Tool-action
  3. C. Synonym
  4. D. Opposite meaning
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. Tool-action

Explanation: The pair Pen:Write follows the relationship 'Tool-action'. This type of relationship helps solve analogy questions quickly.

Shortcut: Name the relation first; do not jump to options.

Q19. Classify the number 49.

Number System basicsplace valueHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Even
  2. B. Odd only
  3. C. Co-prime only
  4. D. Composite
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Composite

Explanation: Check divisibility up to √49. Since a divisor is found, 49 is composite.

Shortcut: To test prime, check only divisors up to square root.

Q20. In the number 7537, what is the place value of the digit 5?

Place valuedivisibilityEasyConcept Practice
  1. A. 50
  2. B. 5
  3. C. 500
  4. D. 5000
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 500

Explanation: Place value means digit multiplied by its position value. In 7537, digit 5 is at the 100 place, so place value = 5 × 100 = 500.

Shortcut: Read from right: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands. Multiply the digit by its place.

Q21. What is the unit digit of 12^45?

Even/odd numbersunit digitMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 2
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 6
  4. D. 8
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 2

Explanation: Unit digits follow cycles. For base 12, calculate powers modulo 10. Since 45 is reduced within the unit-digit cycle, the unit digit is 2.

Shortcut: Use the unit digit cycle; never expand the full power.

Q22. Find the remainder when (8 × 40 + 1) is divided by 6.

Prime/composite numbersremainderMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 0
  2. B. 3
  3. C. 4
  4. D. 5
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 3

Explanation: First calculate 8 × 40 + 1 = 321. Divide by 6. The remainder is 3.

Shortcut: For remainder questions, reduce each term by the divisor before multiplying.

Q23. Using divisibility rules, which statement is true for 115?

Divisibility rulesprime/compositeHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. divisible by 9
  2. B. not divisible by 3
  3. C. multiple of 11
  4. D. prime number
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. not divisible by 3

Explanation: Sum of digits of 115 is 7. A number is divisible by 3 when the digit sum is divisible by 3. Therefore, 115 is not divisible by 3.

Shortcut: For 3 and 9, check digit sum instead of division.

Unit digit basicsplace valueEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style

  2. B. although
  3. C. and
  4. D. therefore

Explanation: 'Although' shows contrast between rain and continuing practice.

Shortcut: Use although/though when the second part is surprising or contrasting.

Q27. If each letter is shifted forward by 3, what will N become?

Verbal: 10 vocabulary wordsalphabet shiftHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Q
  2. B. K
  3. C. R
  4. D. S
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Q

Explanation: Move 3 positions ahead in the alphabet from N. The result is Q.

Shortcut: Write alphabet positions A=1, B=2, ..., Z=26.

  4. D. 31

End-of-Day Revision Card — Day 1

10-point summary

  1. Revise the definition and purpose of Number System basics.
  2. Write formulas once by hand before attempting speed questions.
  3. Solve easy questions first to build accuracy.
  4. Use option elimination wherever full calculation is long.
  5. Track every mistake in the weak-area dashboard.
  6. Reattempt wrong quiz questions without seeing the answer.
  7. Mark completed topics only after solving examples.
  8. Keep verbal practice short but consistent.
  9. Use shortcuts only after understanding the concept.
  10. End the day by reviewing tomorrow’s topic list.

Formula recap

  • Divisibility by 2: Last digit is even — Use for quick even/odd and factor checks.
  • Divisibility by 3: Sum of digits divisible by 3 — Use before doing long division.
  • Divisibility by 9: Sum of digits divisible by 9 — Useful for remainders and shortcuts.
  • Subject-verb agreement: Singular subject takes singular verb — Check subject before verb.
  • Article usage: a/an/the — Use an before vowel sound.

Tricks recap

  • Number System basics: Before solving Number System basics, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • Place value: Before solving Place value, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • Even/odd numbers: Before solving Even/odd numbers, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • Prime/composite numbers: Before solving Prime/composite numbers, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Mistake checklist

  • Wrong base value
  • Wrong unit conversion
  • Misread option
  • Skipped condition
  • No revision of incorrect answers

Weak-area revision advice: Any topic below 60% must be revised before moving to hard questions.

Tomorrow’s preparation preview: Day 2 — LCM, HCF + Sentence Correction.

Day 02 • 28 May 2026

Day 2 — LCM, HCF + Sentence Correction

Study objective: Build practical command over LCM, HCF, Factors and multiples, Co-prime numbers and apply it in TCS NQT-style timed questions.

Previous DayNext Day
Day mini TOC: LCM HCF Factors and multiples Co-prime numbers Highest power in factorial Applications of LCM/HCF Verbal: Sentence correction basics Verbal: Articles and prepositions PracticeQuizScoreRevision

Topics covered

  • LCM
  • HCF
  • Factors and multiples
  • Co-prime numbers
  • Highest power in factorial
  • Applications of LCM/HCF
  • Verbal: Sentence correction basics
  • Verbal: Articles and prepositions
LCM

LCM helps solve questions involving repetition, common multiples, common factors, factorial powers, and schedules. LCM answers 'when will things meet together again', while HCF answers 'largest common division possible'. Learn to identify whether the question asks for a common multiple or common factor.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to LCM.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving LCM, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for LCM; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember LCM schedule trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in LCM.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in LCM is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use LCM schedule trick, HCF division trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: Find whether 729 is divisible by 9.
Digit sum = 7+2+9 = 18. Since 18 is divisible by 9, 729 is divisible by 9.
Shortcut: Digit sum rule avoids division.
Example 2: Find LCM of 12 and 18.
HCF is 6. LCM = 12×18/6 = 36.
Shortcut: Use product/HCF.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting LCM without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.
HCF

HCF helps solve questions involving repetition, common multiples, common factors, factorial powers, and schedules. LCM answers 'when will things meet together again', while HCF answers 'largest common division possible'. Learn to identify whether the question asks for a common multiple or common factor.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to HCF.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving HCF, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for HCF; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember LCM schedule trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in HCF.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in HCF is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use LCM schedule trick, HCF division trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: Find whether 729 is divisible by 9.
Digit sum = 7+2+9 = 18. Since 18 is divisible by 9, 729 is divisible by 9.
Shortcut: Digit sum rule avoids division.
Example 2: Find LCM of 12 and 18.
HCF is 6. LCM = 12×18/6 = 36.
Shortcut: Use product/HCF.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting HCF without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.

Highest power in factorial

Highest power in factorial builds programming logic for TCS NQT coding and MCQ rounds. Focus on dry runs, variable updates, loop boundaries, array indexing, string traversal, and base conditions in recursion.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Highest power in factorial.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Highest power in factorial, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Highest power in factorial; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember Dry run trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Highest power in factorial.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Highest power in factorial is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Dry run trick, Boundary check trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: for i=1 to 3, add i to sum.
sum starts 0. After i=1 sum=1, i=2 sum=3, i=3 sum=6. Output 6.
Shortcut: Make trace table.
Example 2: Check if 'madam' is palindrome.
Reverse is 'madam', same as original, so palindrome.
Shortcut: Compare both ends.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Highest power in factorial without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.
Applications of LCM/HCF

Applications of LCM/HCF covers AP, GP, ratios, and advanced number patterns. Identify whether numbers are increasing by addition or multiplication. Use formulas only after recognizing the pattern.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Applications of LCM/HCF.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Applications of LCM/HCF, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Applications of LCM/HCF; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember AP difference trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Applications of LCM/HCF.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Applications of LCM/HCF is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use AP difference trick, GP ratio trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: Probability of getting even number on a die.
Even outcomes = 2,4,6 = 3. Total = 6. Probability = 3/6 = 1/2.
Shortcut: Favourable/total.
Example 2: AP: a=5, d=3, find 6th term.
T6 = 5+(6-1)×3 = 20.
Shortcut: Use nth term formula.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Applications of LCM/HCF without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.
Verbal: Sentence correction basics

Verbal: Sentence correction basics improves comprehension, grammar, and vocabulary. Read the sentence meaning first, then check grammar rules. In comprehension, answer only from the passage, not from personal knowledge.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Verbal: Sentence correction basics.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Verbal: Sentence correction basics, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Verbal: Sentence correction basics; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember Elimination trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Verbal: Sentence correction basics.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Verbal: Sentence correction basics is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Elimination trick, Connector trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: Choose correct: 'He go' or 'He goes'.
'He goes' is correct because singular subject uses singular verb.
Shortcut: Check subject first.
Example 2: Synonym of rapid.
Rapid means fast or quick.
Shortcut: Use context clue.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Verbal: Sentence correction basics without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.

Important Formulas and Rules

Formula/RuleExpressionWhen to use
LCM × HCFFor two numbers: LCM × HCF = productUse when HCF or LCM is known.
Highest power in n!⌊n/p⌋ + ⌊n/p²⌋ + ...Use for factorial prime powers.
Co-primeHCF = 1Numbers need not both be prime.
Loop dry runTrack variable after each iterationUse a small table.
Moduloa % b = remainderUseful for digit and divisibility code.
Array traversalIndex 0 to n-1Avoid out-of-bound errors.
AP nth terma + (n-1)dCommon difference.
AP sumn/2[2a+(n-1)d]Sum of AP terms.
GP nth terma×r^(n-1)Common ratio.
Subject-verb agreementSingular subject takes singular verbCheck subject before verb.
Article usagea/an/theUse an before vowel sound.
PrepositionsDepend on verb phraseLearn common pairs.

Practice Question Bank — 65 Questions

All questions below are original TCS NQT PYQ-style or concept-practice questions with answers and explanations.

Q1. Find the HCF of 49 and 75.

LCMHCFMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 9
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 7
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 1

Explanation: Divide both numbers by common factors or use Euclid's method. The greatest common divisor of 49 and 75 is 1.

Shortcut: Use Euclid: repeatedly replace larger number by remainder.

Q2. What is the highest power of 4 in 7! ?

HCFfactorsMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 0
  3. C. 2
  4. D. 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 1

Explanation: Use Legendre formula: floor(7/4) + floor(7/16) + ... = 1. Therefore highest power is 1.

Shortcut: For factorial powers, keep dividing n by p, p², p³.

Q3. Numbers 18 and 21 are:

Factors and multiplesco-primeHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Both prime
  2. B. LCM is 1
  3. C. Both even
  4. D. Not co-prime
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Not co-prime

Explanation: Two numbers are co-prime if their HCF is 1. HCF(18,21) = 3, so they are not co-prime.

Shortcut: Co-prime means common factor only 1; numbers need not both be prime.

Q4. Two bells ring every 8 minutes and 8 minutes. If they ring together now, after how many minutes will they ring together again?

Co-prime numbersfactorial powerEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 16
  2. B. 8
  3. C. 24
  4. D. 0
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 8

Explanation: Events repeating together use LCM. LCM(8,8) = 8, so bells meet again after 8 minutes.

Shortcut: Whenever schedules repeat together, use LCM.

Q5. What is the time complexity of a single loop that visits each element of an array once?

Highest power in factorialoutput predictionMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. O(n²)
  2. B. O(log n)
  3. C. O(1)
  4. D. O(n)
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. O(n)

Explanation: A single loop over n elements performs work proportional to n. Therefore time complexity is O(n).

Shortcut: One full traversal usually means O(n).

Q6. Simplify the ratio 18:20.

Applications of LCM/HCFGPMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 9:10
  2. B. 1:1
  3. C. 18:20
  4. D. 20:18
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 9:10

Explanation: Divide both terms by HCF 2. Simplified ratio = 9:10.

Shortcut: Always reduce ratio by HCF.

Q7. Choose the word closest in meaning to 'kind and generous'.

Verbal: Sentence correction basicssentence fitHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. hostile
  2. B. benevolent
  3. C. ordinary
  4. D. careless
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. benevolent

Explanation: 'Benevolent' means kind, generous, and willing to help others.

Shortcut: Break benevolent as 'benefit-giving' in memory.

Q8. Fill in the blank: ___ it was raining, the team continued the practice.

Verbal: Articles and prepositionsreading inferenceEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. because
  2. B. although
  3. C. and
  4. D. therefore
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. although

Explanation: 'Although' shows contrast between rain and continuing practice.

Shortcut: Use although/though when the second part is surprising or contrasting.

Q9. Two bells ring every 8 minutes and 8 minutes. If they ring together now, after how many minutes will they ring together again?

LCMfactorial powerMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 16
  2. B. 8
  3. C. 24
  4. D. 0
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 8

Explanation: Events repeating together use LCM. LCM(8,8) = 8, so bells meet again after 8 minutes.

Shortcut: Whenever schedules repeat together, use LCM.

Q10. Find the LCM of 14 and 26.

HCFLCMMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 184
  2. B. 182
  3. C. 40
  4. D. 2
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 182

Explanation: HCF(14,26) = 2. LCM × HCF = product of numbers. LCM = 14 × 26 ÷ 2 = 182.

Shortcut: Use LCM × HCF = product for two numbers.

Q11. Find the HCF of 59 and 79.

Factors and multiplesHCFHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 9
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 7
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 1

Explanation: Divide both numbers by common factors or use Euclid's method. The greatest common divisor of 59 and 79 is 1.

Shortcut: Use Euclid: repeatedly replace larger number by remainder.

Q12. What is the highest power of 4 in 7! ?

Co-prime numbersfactorsEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 0
  3. C. 2
  4. D. 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 1

Explanation: Use Legendre formula: floor(7/4) + floor(7/16) + ... = 1. Therefore highest power is 1.

Shortcut: For factorial powers, keep dividing n by p, p², p³.

Q13. A loop counts even numbers in [2, 3, 4, 5, 6]. What count is printed?

Highest power in factorialloop traceMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 20
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 2
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 3

Explanation: Even numbers are 2, 4 and 6, so the count is 3.

Shortcut: For count problems, increase counter only when condition is true.

Q14. Simplify the ratio 26:20.

Applications of LCM/HCFmixed quantMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 20:26
  2. B. 26:20
  3. C. 1:1
  4. D. 13:10
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 13:10

Explanation: Divide both terms by HCF 2. Simplified ratio = 13:10.

Shortcut: Always reduce ratio by HCF.

Q15. Choose the correct passive voice sentence.

Verbal: Sentence correction basicsgrammarHardConcept Practice
  1. A. The report has submit by Anil.
  2. B. The report were submitted by Anil.
  3. C. The report was submitted by Anil.
  4. D. The report submitted by Anil.
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. The report was submitted by Anil.

Explanation: Singular subject 'report' takes 'was'. Past participle of submit is 'submitted'.

Shortcut: Passive voice pattern: object + be verb + past participle.

Q16. Para-jumble: (1) and improves accuracy. (2) Daily reading builds speed. (3) It also increases vocabulary. (4) Therefore it helps in verbal tests. Choose the best order.

Verbal: Articles and prepositionsvocabularyEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 3-2-1-4
  2. B. 1-2-3-4
  3. C. 2-1-4-3
  4. D. Arrange: 2-3-1-4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Arrange: 2-3-1-4

Explanation: Sentence 2 introduces daily reading. Sentence 3 adds vocabulary benefit. Sentence 1 continues another benefit. Sentence 4 concludes. Order is 2-3-1-4.

Shortcut: Find opening sentence first, then connectors like also/therefore.

Q17. What is the highest power of 4 in 7! ?

LCMfactorsMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 0
  3. C. 2
  4. D. 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 1

Explanation: Use Legendre formula: floor(7/4) + floor(7/16) + ... = 1. Therefore highest power is 1.

Shortcut: For factorial powers, keep dividing n by p, p², p³.

Q18. Numbers 33 and 22 are:

HCFco-primeMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Both prime
  2. B. LCM is 1
  3. C. Both even
  4. D. Not co-prime
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Not co-prime

Explanation: Two numbers are co-prime if their HCF is 1. HCF(33,22) = 11, so they are not co-prime.

Shortcut: Co-prime means common factor only 1; numbers need not both be prime.

Q19. Two bells ring every 8 minutes and 8 minutes. If they ring together now, after how many minutes will they ring together again?

Factors and multiplesfactorial powerHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 16
  2. B. 8
  3. C. 24
  4. D. 0
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 8

Explanation: Events repeating together use LCM. LCM(8,8) = 8, so bells meet again after 8 minutes.

Shortcut: Whenever schedules repeat together, use LCM.

Q20. Find the LCM of 16 and 24.

Co-prime numbersLCMEasyConcept Practice
  1. A. 48
  2. B. 8
  3. C. 56
  4. D. 40
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 48

Explanation: HCF(16,24) = 8. LCM × HCF = product of numbers. LCM = 16 × 24 ÷ 8 = 48.

Shortcut: Use LCM × HCF = product for two numbers.

Q21. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=4; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

Highest power in factorialdebuggingMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 10
  2. B. 16
  3. C. 9
  4. D. 11
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 10

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 4. Sum = 4×(4+1)/2 = 10. Output is 10.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

Q22. Simplify the ratio 34:20.

Applications of LCM/HCFnumber theoryMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 17:10
  2. B. 34:20
  3. C. 20:34
  4. D. 1:1
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 17:10

Explanation: Divide both terms by HCF 2. Simplified ratio = 17:10.

Shortcut: Always reduce ratio by HCF.

Q23. In reading comprehension, what do we call a conclusion drawn from clues in the passage?

Verbal: Sentence correction basicsreading inferenceHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. pronunciation
  2. B. calculation
  3. C. inference
  4. D. spelling
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. inference

Explanation: A conclusion based on clues and logic from the passage is called an inference.

Shortcut: Inference = information implied, not directly stated.

Q24. Choose the grammatically correct sentence.

Verbal: Articles and prepositionserror spottingEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. The manager discussed the plan with the team.
  2. B. The manager discuss the plan with the team.
  3. C. The manager discussed about the plan with the team.
  4. D. The manager discussed the plan to the team.
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. The manager discussed the plan with the team.

Explanation: 'Discuss' is not followed by 'about' in standard usage. The correct form is 'discussed the plan with the team'.

Shortcut: After discuss, avoid unnecessary 'about'.

Q25. Find the LCM of 26 and 25.

LCMLCMMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 651
  2. B. 650
  3. C. 1
  4. D. 51
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 650

Explanation: HCF(26,25) = 1. LCM × HCF = product of numbers. LCM = 26 × 25 ÷ 1 = 650.

Shortcut: Use LCM × HCF = product for two numbers.

Q26. Find the HCF of 74 and 76.

HCFHCFMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 8
  2. B. 1
  3. C. 2
  4. D. 4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 2

Explanation: Divide both numbers by common factors or use Euclid's method. The greatest common divisor of 74 and 76 is 2.

Shortcut: Use Euclid: repeatedly replace larger number by remainder.

Q27. What is the highest power of 4 in 7! ?

Factors and multiplesfactorsHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 0
  3. C. 2
  4. D. 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 1

Explanation: Use Legendre formula: floor(7/4) + floor(7/16) + ... = 1. Therefore highest power is 1.

Shortcut: For factorial powers, keep dividing n by p, p², p³.

Q28. Numbers 43 and 18 are:

Co-prime numbersco-primeEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Co-prime
  2. B. Both prime
  3. C. Both even
  4. D. LCM is 1
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Co-prime

Explanation: Two numbers are co-prime if their HCF is 1. HCF(43,18) = 1, so they are co-prime.

Shortcut: Co-prime means common factor only 1; numbers need not both be prime.

Q29. What is the value of 9 % 4 in C-style programming?

Highest power in factorialarray/string logicMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 2
  2. B. 36
  3. C. 1
  4. D. 13
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 1

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 9 divided by 4 leaves remainder 1.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q30. Simplify the ratio 42:20.

Applications of LCM/HCFAPMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 20:42
  2. B. 21:10
  3. C. 42:20
  4. D. 1:1
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 21:10

Explanation: Divide both terms by HCF 2. Simplified ratio = 21:10.

Shortcut: Always reduce ratio by HCF.

Q31. Choose the word closest in meaning to 'kind and generous'.

Verbal: Sentence correction basicsvocabularyHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. hostile
  2. B. benevolent
  3. C. ordinary
  4. D. careless
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. benevolent

Explanation: 'Benevolent' means kind, generous, and willing to help others.

Shortcut: Break benevolent as 'benefit-giving' in memory.

Q32. Fill in the blank: ___ it was raining, the team continued the practice.

Verbal: Articles and prepositionssentence fitEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. because
  2. B. although
  3. C. and
  4. D. therefore
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. although

Explanation: 'Although' shows contrast between rain and continuing practice.

Shortcut: Use although/though when the second part is surprising or contrasting.

Q33. Numbers 48 and 23 are:

LCMco-primeMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Co-prime
  2. B. Both prime
  3. C. Both even
  4. D. LCM is 1
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Co-prime

Explanation: Two numbers are co-prime if their HCF is 1. HCF(48,23) = 1, so they are co-prime.

Shortcut: Co-prime means common factor only 1; numbers need not both be prime.

Q34. Two bells ring every 8 minutes and 8 minutes. If they ring together now, after how many minutes will they ring together again?

HCFfactorial powerMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 16
  2. B. 8
  3. C. 24
  4. D. 0
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 8

Explanation: Events repeating together use LCM. LCM(8,8) = 8, so bells meet again after 8 minutes.

Shortcut: Whenever schedules repeat together, use LCM.

Q35. Find the LCM of 28 and 27.

Factors and multiplesLCMHardConcept Practice
  1. A. 55
  2. B. 757
  3. C. 756
  4. D. 1
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 756

Explanation: HCF(28,27) = 1. LCM × HCF = product of numbers. LCM = 28 × 27 ÷ 1 = 756.

Shortcut: Use LCM × HCF = product for two numbers.

Q36. Find the HCF of 84 and 74.

Co-prime numbersHCFEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 8
  2. B. 1
  3. C. 2
  4. D. 4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 2

Explanation: Divide both numbers by common factors or use Euclid's method. The greatest common divisor of 84 and 74 is 2.

Shortcut: Use Euclid: repeatedly replace larger number by remainder.

Q37. Given array [3, 4, 9, 4], what value will a maximum-finding loop return?

Highest power in factorialdry runMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 3
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 20
  4. D. 9
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 9

Explanation: Compare each element and keep the largest. The maximum among [3, 4, 9, 4] is 9.

Shortcut: Initialize max with first element, then compare one by one.

Q38. Simplify the ratio 50:20.

Applications of LCM/HCFadvanced ratioMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 20:50
  2. B. 1:1
  3. C. 5:2
  4. D. 50:20
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 5:2

Explanation: Divide both terms by HCF 10. Simplified ratio = 5:2.

Shortcut: Always reduce ratio by HCF.

Q39. Choose the correct passive voice sentence.

Verbal: Sentence correction basicserror spottingHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. The report has submit by Anil.
  2. B. The report were submitted by Anil.
  3. C. The report was submitted by Anil.
  4. D. The report submitted by Anil.
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. The report was submitted by Anil.

Explanation: Singular subject 'report' takes 'was'. Past participle of submit is 'submitted'.

Shortcut: Passive voice pattern: object + be verb + past participle.

Q40. Para-jumble: (1) and improves accuracy. (2) Daily reading builds speed. (3) It also increases vocabulary. (4) Therefore it helps in verbal tests. Choose the best order.

Verbal: Articles and prepositionsgrammarEasyConcept Practice
  1. A. 3-2-1-4
  2. B. 1-2-3-4
  3. C. 2-1-4-3
  4. D. Arrange: 2-3-1-4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Arrange: 2-3-1-4

Explanation: Sentence 2 introduces daily reading. Sentence 3 adds vocabulary benefit. Sentence 1 continues another benefit. Sentence 4 concludes. Order is 2-3-1-4.

Shortcut: Find opening sentence first, then connectors like also/therefore.

Q41. Find the HCF of 89 and 79.

LCMHCFMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 9
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 7
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 1

Explanation: Divide both numbers by common factors or use Euclid's method. The greatest common divisor of 89 and 79 is 1.

Shortcut: Use Euclid: repeatedly replace larger number by remainder.

Q42. What is the highest power of 4 in 7! ?

HCFfactorsMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 0
  3. C. 2
  4. D. 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 1

Explanation: Use Legendre formula: floor(7/4) + floor(7/16) + ... = 1. Therefore highest power is 1.

Shortcut: For factorial powers, keep dividing n by p, p², p³.

Q43. Numbers 58 and 19 are:

Factors and multiplesco-primeHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Co-prime
  2. B. Both prime
  3. C. Both even
  4. D. LCM is 1
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Co-prime

Explanation: Two numbers are co-prime if their HCF is 1. HCF(58,19) = 1, so they are co-prime.

Shortcut: Co-prime means common factor only 1; numbers need not both be prime.

Q44. Two bells ring every 8 minutes and 8 minutes. If they ring together now, after how many minutes will they ring together again?

Co-prime numbersfactorial powerEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 16
  2. B. 8
  3. C. 24
  4. D. 0
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 8

Explanation: Events repeating together use LCM. LCM(8,8) = 8, so bells meet again after 8 minutes.

Shortcut: Whenever schedules repeat together, use LCM.

Q45. Classify the string 'tcs' after comparing it with its reverse.

Highest power in factorialoutput predictionMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. Not palindrome
  2. B. Anagram
  3. C. Empty string
  4. D. Sorted string
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Not palindrome

Explanation: Reverse of 'tcs' is 'sct'. Since it does not match, it is not palindrome.

Shortcut: Palindrome check: compare first with last, second with second last.

Q46. Simplify the ratio 58:20.

Applications of LCM/HCFGPMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 20:58
  2. B. 58:20
  3. C. 29:10
  4. D. 1:1
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 29:10

Explanation: Divide both terms by HCF 2. Simplified ratio = 29:10.

Shortcut: Always reduce ratio by HCF.

Q47. In reading comprehension, what do we call a conclusion drawn from clues in the passage?

Verbal: Sentence correction basicssentence fitHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. pronunciation
  2. B. calculation
  3. C. inference
  4. D. spelling
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. inference

Explanation: A conclusion based on clues and logic from the passage is called an inference.

Shortcut: Inference = information implied, not directly stated.

Q48. Choose the grammatically correct sentence.

Verbal: Articles and prepositionsreading inferenceEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. The manager discussed the plan with the team.
  2. B. The manager discuss the plan with the team.
  3. C. The manager discussed about the plan with the team.
  4. D. The manager discussed the plan to the team.
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. The manager discussed the plan with the team.

Explanation: 'Discuss' is not followed by 'about' in standard usage. The correct form is 'discussed the plan with the team'.

Shortcut: After discuss, avoid unnecessary 'about'.

Q49. Two bells ring every 8 minutes and 8 minutes. If they ring together now, after how many minutes will they ring together again?

LCMfactorial powerMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 16
  2. B. 8
  3. C. 24
  4. D. 0
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 8

Explanation: Events repeating together use LCM. LCM(8,8) = 8, so bells meet again after 8 minutes.

Shortcut: Whenever schedules repeat together, use LCM.

Q50. Find the LCM of 22 and 26.

HCFLCMMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 288
  2. B. 48
  3. C. 286
  4. D. 2
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 286

Explanation: HCF(22,26) = 2. LCM × HCF = product of numbers. LCM = 22 × 26 ÷ 2 = 286.

Shortcut: Use LCM × HCF = product for two numbers.

Q51. Find the HCF of 99 and 77.

Factors and multiplesHCFHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 10
  2. B. 11
  3. C. 17
  4. D. 13
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 11

Explanation: Divide both numbers by common factors or use Euclid's method. The greatest common divisor of 99 and 77 is 11.

Shortcut: Use Euclid: repeatedly replace larger number by remainder.

Q52. What is the highest power of 4 in 7! ?

Co-prime numbersfactorsEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 0
  3. C. 2
  4. D. 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 1

Explanation: Use Legendre formula: floor(7/4) + floor(7/16) + ... = 1. Therefore highest power is 1.

Shortcut: For factorial powers, keep dividing n by p, p², p³.

Q53. What does a factorial function return for input 8?

Highest power in factorialloop traceMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 40328
  2. B. 40320
  3. C. 5040
  4. D. 40319
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 40320

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 8. 8! = 40320.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

Q54. Simplify the ratio 66:20.

Applications of LCM/HCFmixed quantMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 33:10
  2. B. 66:20
  3. C. 1:1
  4. D. 20:66
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 33:10

Explanation: Divide both terms by HCF 2. Simplified ratio = 33:10.

Shortcut: Always reduce ratio by HCF.

Q55. Choose the word closest in meaning to 'kind and generous'.

Verbal: Sentence correction basicsgrammarHardConcept Practice
  1. A. hostile
  2. B. benevolent
  3. C. ordinary
  4. D. careless
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. benevolent

Explanation: 'Benevolent' means kind, generous, and willing to help others.

Shortcut: Break benevolent as 'benefit-giving' in memory.

Q56. Fill in the blank: ___ it was raining, the team continued the practice.

Verbal: Articles and prepositionsvocabularyEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. because
  2. B. although
  3. C. and
  4. D. therefore
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. although

Explanation: 'Although' shows contrast between rain and continuing practice.

Shortcut: Use although/though when the second part is surprising or contrasting.

Q57. What is the highest power of 4 in 7! ?

LCMfactorsMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 0
  3. C. 2
  4. D. 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 1

Explanation: Use Legendre formula: floor(7/4) + floor(7/16) + ... = 1. Therefore highest power is 1.

Shortcut: For factorial powers, keep dividing n by p, p², p³.

Q58. Numbers 73 and 20 are:

HCFco-primeMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Co-prime
  2. B. Both prime
  3. C. Both even
  4. D. LCM is 1
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Co-prime

Explanation: Two numbers are co-prime if their HCF is 1. HCF(73,20) = 1, so they are co-prime.

Shortcut: Co-prime means common factor only 1; numbers need not both be prime.

Q59. Two bells ring every 8 minutes and 8 minutes. If they ring together now, after how many minutes will they ring together again?

Factors and multiplesfactorial powerHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 16
  2. B. 8
  3. C. 24
  4. D. 0
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 8

Explanation: Events repeating together use LCM. LCM(8,8) = 8, so bells meet again after 8 minutes.

Shortcut: Whenever schedules repeat together, use LCM.

Q60. Find the LCM of 24 and 24.

Co-prime numbersLCMEasyConcept Practice
  1. A. 40
  2. B. 24
  3. C. 48
  4. D. 32
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 24

Explanation: HCF(24,24) = 24. LCM × HCF = product of numbers. LCM = 24 × 24 ÷ 24 = 24.

Shortcut: Use LCM × HCF = product for two numbers.

Q61. What is the time complexity of a single loop that visits each element of an array once?

Highest power in factorialdebuggingMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. O(n²)
  2. B. O(log n)
  3. C. O(1)
  4. D. O(n)
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. O(n)

Explanation: A single loop over n elements performs work proportional to n. Therefore time complexity is O(n).

Shortcut: One full traversal usually means O(n).

Q62. Simplify the ratio 74:20.

Applications of LCM/HCFnumber theoryMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1:1
  2. B. 37:10
  3. C. 74:20
  4. D. 20:74
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 37:10

Explanation: Divide both terms by HCF 2. Simplified ratio = 37:10.

Shortcut: Always reduce ratio by HCF.

Q63. Choose the correct passive voice sentence.

Verbal: Sentence correction basicsreading inferenceHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. The report has submit by Anil.
  2. B. The report were submitted by Anil.
  3. C. The report was submitted by Anil.
  4. D. The report submitted by Anil.
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. The report was submitted by Anil.

Explanation: Singular subject 'report' takes 'was'. Past participle of submit is 'submitted'.

Shortcut: Passive voice pattern: object + be verb + past participle.

Q64. Para-jumble: (1) and improves accuracy. (2) Daily reading builds speed. (3) It also increases vocabulary. (4) Therefore it helps in verbal tests. Choose the best order.

Verbal: Articles and prepositionserror spottingEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 3-2-1-4
  2. B. 1-2-3-4
  3. C. 2-1-4-3
  4. D. Arrange: 2-3-1-4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Arrange: 2-3-1-4

Explanation: Sentence 2 introduces daily reading. Sentence 3 adds vocabulary benefit. Sentence 1 continues another benefit. Sentence 4 concludes. Order is 2-3-1-4.

Shortcut: Find opening sentence first, then connectors like also/therefore.

Q65. Find the LCM of 16 and 25.

LCMLCMMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 41
  2. B. 401
  3. C. 400
  4. D. 1
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 400

Explanation: HCF(16,25) = 1. LCM × HCF = product of numbers. LCM = 16 × 25 ÷ 1 = 400.

Shortcut: Use LCM × HCF = product for two numbers.

Day 2 Score Analyzer

Correct: 0

Accuracy: 0%

Recommendation: Submit the quiz to get a personalized recommendation.

End-of-Day Revision Card — Day 2

10-point summary

  1. Revise the definition and purpose of LCM.
  2. Write formulas once by hand before attempting speed questions.
  3. Solve easy questions first to build accuracy.
  4. Use option elimination wherever full calculation is long.
  5. Track every mistake in the weak-area dashboard.
  6. Reattempt wrong quiz questions without seeing the answer.
  7. Mark completed topics only after solving examples.
  8. Keep verbal practice short but consistent.
  9. Use shortcuts only after understanding the concept.
  10. End the day by reviewing tomorrow’s topic list.

Formula recap

  • LCM × HCF: For two numbers: LCM × HCF = product — Use when HCF or LCM is known.
  • Highest power in n!: ⌊n/p⌋ + ⌊n/p²⌋ + ... — Use for factorial prime powers.
  • Co-prime: HCF = 1 — Numbers need not both be prime.
  • Loop dry run: Track variable after each iteration — Use a small table.
  • Modulo: a % b = remainder — Useful for digit and divisibility code.

Tricks recap

  • LCM: Before solving LCM, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • HCF: Before solving HCF, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • Factors and multiples: Before solving Factors and multiples, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • Co-prime numbers: Before solving Co-prime numbers, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Mistake checklist

  • Wrong base value
  • Wrong unit conversion
  • Misread option
  • Skipped condition
  • No revision of incorrect answers

Weak-area revision advice: Any topic below 60% must be revised before moving to hard questions.

Tomorrow’s preparation preview: Day 3 — Percentage + Vocabulary Practice.

Day 03 • 29 May 2026

Day 3 — Percentage + Vocabulary Practice

Study objective: Build practical command over Percentage basics, Fraction to percentage, Percentage to fraction, Increase/decrease and apply it in TCS NQT-style timed questions.

Previous DayNext Day
Day mini TOC: Percentage basics Fraction to percentage Percentage to fraction Increase/decrease Successive percentage Percentage comparison Verbal: Fill in the blanks Verbal: Synonyms and antonyms PracticeQuizScoreRevision

Topics covered

  • Percentage basics
  • Fraction to percentage
  • Percentage to fraction
  • Increase/decrease
  • Successive percentage
  • Percentage comparison
  • Verbal: Fill in the blanks
  • Verbal: Synonyms and antonyms
Percentage basics

Percentage basics converts a comparison into parts out of 100. It is used in marks, salary, population, profit-loss, discount, and data interpretation. Always identify the base value first, because percentage increase/decrease is calculated on the original value.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Percentage basics.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Percentage basics, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Percentage basics; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember Percentage base trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Percentage basics.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Percentage basics is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Percentage base trick, 10%-5%-1% trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: Find 15% of 240.
10% = 24 and 5% = 12, so 15% = 36.
Shortcut: Break into 10% + 5%.
Example 2: A value increases from 80 to 100. Find increase %.
Increase = 20. Increase % = 20/80×100 = 25%.
Shortcut: Base is original value.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Percentage basics without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.
Fraction to percentage

  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Successive percentage

Successive percentage converts a comparison into parts out of 100. It is used in marks, salary, population, profit-loss, discount, and data interpretation. Always identify the base value first, because percentage increase/decrease is calculated on the original value.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Successive percentage.

  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Successive percentage, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Successive percentage; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember Percentage base trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Successive percentage.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Successive percentage is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Percentage base trick, 10%-5%-1% trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: Find 15% of 240.
10% = 24 and 5% = 12, so 15% = 36.
Shortcut: Break into 10% + 5%.
Example 2: A value increases from 80 to 100. Find increase %.
Increase = 20. Increase % = 20/80×100 = 25%.
Shortcut: Base is original value.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Successive percentage without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.
Percentage comparison

Percentage comparison converts a comparison into parts out of 100. It is used in marks, salary, population, profit-loss, discount, and data interpretation. Always identify the base value first, because percentage increase/decrease is calculated on the original value.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Percentage comparison.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Percentage comparison, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Percentage comparison; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember Percentage base trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Percentage comparison.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Percentage comparison is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Percentage base trick, 10%-5%-1% trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: Find 15% of 240.
10% = 24 and 5% = 12, so 15% = 36.
Shortcut: Break into 10% + 5%.
Example 2: A value increases from 80 to 100. Find increase %.
Increase = 20. Increase % = 20/80×100 = 25%.
Shortcut: Base is original value.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Percentage comparison without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.
Verbal: Fill in the blanks

Verbal: Fill in the blanks improves comprehension, grammar, and vocabulary. Read the sentence meaning first, then check grammar rules. In comprehension, answer only from the passage, not from personal knowledge.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Verbal: Fill in the blanks.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Verbal: Fill in the blanks, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Verbal: Fill in the blanks; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember Elimination trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Verbal: Fill in the blanks.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Verbal: Fill in the blanks is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Elimination trick, Connector trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: Choose correct: 'He go' or 'He goes'.
'He goes' is correct because singular subject uses singular verb.
Shortcut: Check subject first.
Example 2: Synonym of rapid.
Rapid means fast or quick.
Shortcut: Use context clue.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Verbal: Fill in the blanks without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.
Verbal: Synonyms and antonyms

Verbal: Synonyms and antonyms improves comprehension, grammar, and vocabulary. Read the sentence meaning first, then check grammar rules. In comprehension, answer only from the passage, not from personal knowledge.
