Q35. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=3; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

Sorting dry runoutput predictionHardConcept Practice
  1. A. 9
  2. B. 5
  3. C. 6
  4. D. 7
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 6

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 3. Sum = 3×(3+1)/2 = 6. Output is 6.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

Q36. What is the unit digit of 11^79?

Time complexity basicsunit digitEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 7
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 5
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 1

Explanation: Unit digits follow cycles. For base 11, calculate powers modulo 10. Since 79 is reduced within the unit-digit cycle, the unit digit is 1.

Shortcut: Use the unit digit cycle; never expand the full power.

Q37. Given array [1, 4, 9, 4], what value will a maximum-finding loop return?

Sorting basicsdry runMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 9
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 1
  4. D. 18
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 9

Explanation: Compare each element and keep the largest. The maximum among [1, 4, 9, 4] is 9.

Shortcut: Initialize max with first element, then compare one by one.

Bubble sortloop traceMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Palindrome
  2. B. Empty string
  3. C. Anagram
  4. D. Sorted string
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Palindrome

Explanation: Reverse of 'level' is 'level'. Since it matches, it is palindrome.

Shortcut: Palindrome check: compare first with last, second with second last.

Shortcut: For remainder questions, reduce each term by the divisor before multiplying.

End-of-Day Revision Card — Day 20

10-point summary

  1. Revise the definition and purpose of Sorting basics.
  2. Write formulas once by hand before attempting speed questions.
  3. Solve easy questions first to build accuracy.
  4. Use option elimination wherever full calculation is long.
  5. Track every mistake in the weak-area dashboard.
  6. Reattempt wrong quiz questions without seeing the answer.
  7. Mark completed topics only after solving examples.
  8. Keep verbal practice short but consistent.
  9. Use shortcuts only after understanding the concept.
  10. End the day by reviewing tomorrow’s topic list.

Formula recap

  • Loop dry run: Track variable after each iteration — Use a small table.
  • Modulo: a % b = remainder — Useful for digit and divisibility code.
  • Array traversal: Index 0 to n-1 — Avoid out-of-bound errors.
  • Divisibility by 2: Last digit is even — Use for quick even/odd and factor checks.
  • Divisibility by 3: Sum of digits divisible by 3 — Use before doing long division.

Tricks recap

  • Sorting basics: Before solving Sorting basics, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • Bubble sort: Before solving Bubble sort, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • Selection sort: Before solving Selection sort, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • Insertion sort basics: Before solving Insertion sort basics, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Mistake checklist

  • Wrong base value
  • Wrong unit conversion
  • Misread option
  • Skipped condition
  • No revision of incorrect answers

Weak-area revision advice: Any topic below 60% must be revised before moving to hard questions.

Tomorrow’s preparation preview: Day 21 — Recursion Basics.

Day 21 • 16 June 2026

Day 21 — Recursion Basics

Study objective: Build practical command over Recursion basics, Base condition, Recursive calls, Factorial and apply it in TCS NQT-style timed questions.

Previous DayNext Day

Topics covered

  • Recursion basics
  • Base condition
  • Recursive calls
  • Factorial
  • Fibonacci
  • Sum of digits
  • Recursion dry run
Recursion basics

Recursion basics builds programming logic for TCS NQT coding and MCQ rounds. Focus on dry runs, variable updates, loop boundaries, array indexing, string traversal, and base conditions in recursion.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Recursion basics.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Recursion basics, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Recursion basics; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember Dry run trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Recursion basics.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Recursion basics is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Dry run trick, Boundary check trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: for i=1 to 3, add i to sum.
sum starts 0. After i=1 sum=1, i=2 sum=3, i=3 sum=6. Output 6.
Shortcut: Make trace table.
Example 2: Check if 'madam' is palindrome.
Reverse is 'madam', same as original, so palindrome.
Shortcut: Compare both ends.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Recursion basics without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.
Base condition

Base condition is the foundation of quantitative aptitude. Start by understanding what a number represents, how digits contribute through place value, and how divisibility or remainder rules reduce calculation. In TCS NQT, these questions are usually short but accuracy matters. Solve by checking the structure of the number before doing long calculation.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Base condition.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Base condition, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Base condition; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember Last digit trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Base condition.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Base condition is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Last digit trick, Unit digit trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: Find whether 729 is divisible by 9.
Digit sum = 7+2+9 = 18. Since 18 is divisible by 9, 729 is divisible by 9.
Shortcut: Digit sum rule avoids division.
Example 2: Find LCM of 12 and 18.
HCF is 6. LCM = 12×18/6 = 36.
Shortcut: Use product/HCF.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Base condition without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.

Important Formulas and Rules

Formula/RuleExpressionWhen to use
Loop dry runTrack variable after each iterationUse a small table.
Moduloa % b = remainderUseful for digit and divisibility code.
Array traversalIndex 0 to n-1Avoid out-of-bound errors.
Divisibility by 2Last digit is evenUse for quick even/odd and factor checks.
Divisibility by 3Sum of digits divisible by 3Use before doing long division.
Divisibility by 9Sum of digits divisible by 9Useful for remainders and shortcuts.

Practice Question Bank — 65 Questions

All questions below are original TCS NQT PYQ-style or concept-practice questions with answers and explanations.

Q1. What is the value of 6 % 3 in C-style programming?

Recursion basicsdebuggingMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 0
  2. B. 9
  3. C. 18
  4. D. 2
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 0

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 6 divided by 3 leaves remainder 0.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q2. Find the remainder when (6 × 40 + 21) is divided by 7.

Base conditionremainderMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 5
  2. B. 2
  3. C. 4
  4. D. 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 2

Explanation: First calculate 6 × 40 + 21 = 261. Divide by 7. The remainder is 2.

Shortcut: For remainder questions, reduce each term by the divisor before multiplying.

Q3. Using divisibility rules, which statement is true for 135?

Recursive callsprime/compositeHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. multiple of 11
  2. B. prime number
  3. C. divisible by 9
  4. D. divisible by 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. divisible by 3

Explanation: Sum of digits of 135 is 9. A number is divisible by 3 when the digit sum is divisible by 3. Therefore, 135 is divisible by 3.

Shortcut: For 3 and 9, check digit sum instead of division.

Q4. What does a factorial function return for input 9?

Factorialarray/string logicEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 362889
  2. B. 362879
  3. C. 40320
  4. D. 362880
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 362880

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 9. 9! = 362880.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

Q5. In the number 8142, what is the place value of the digit 1?

FibonaccidivisibilityMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 100
  3. C. 1000
  4. D. 10
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 100

Explanation: Place value means digit multiplied by its position value. In 8142, digit 1 is at the 100 place, so place value = 1 × 100 = 100.

Shortcut: Read from right: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands. Multiply the digit by its place.

Q6. What is the unit digit of 13^50?

Sum of digitsunit digitMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 5
  2. B. 3
  3. C. 1
  4. D. 9
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 9

Explanation: Unit digits follow cycles. For base 13, calculate powers modulo 10. Since 50 is reduced within the unit-digit cycle, the unit digit is 9.

Shortcut: Use the unit digit cycle; never expand the full power.

Q7. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=5; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

Recursion dry rundry runHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 14
  2. B. 16
  3. C. 15
  4. D. 25
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 15

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 5. Sum = 5×(5+1)/2 = 15. Output is 15.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

Q8. What is the value of 8 % 3 in C-style programming?

Recursion basicsloop traceEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 2
  2. B. 24
  3. C. 11
  4. D. 10
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 2

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 8 divided by 3 leaves remainder 2.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q9. Classify the number 59.

Base conditionplace valueMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Odd only
  2. B. Co-prime only
  3. C. Even
  4. D. Prime
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Prime

Explanation: Check divisibility up to √59. Since no divisor is found, 59 is prime.

Shortcut: To test prime, check only divisors up to square root.

Q10. In the number 8187, what is the place value of the digit 1?

Recursive callsdivisibilityMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 100
  3. C. 1000
  4. D. 10
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 100

Explanation: Place value means digit multiplied by its position value. In 8187, digit 1 is at the 100 place, so place value = 1 × 100 = 100.

Shortcut: Read from right: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands. Multiply the digit by its place.

Q11. What does a factorial function return for input 6?

FactorialdebuggingHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 719
  2. B. 720
  3. C. 120
  4. D. 726
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 720

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 6. 6! = 720.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

Q12. Find the remainder when (7 × 50 + 21) is divided by 10.

FibonacciremainderEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 3
  2. B. 1
  3. C. 2
  4. D. 4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 1

Explanation: First calculate 7 × 50 + 21 = 371. Divide by 10. The remainder is 1.

Shortcut: For remainder questions, reduce each term by the divisor before multiplying.

Q13. Using divisibility rules, which statement is true for 145?

Sum of digitsprime/compositeMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. divisible by 9
  2. B. not divisible by 3
  3. C. multiple of 11
  4. D. prime number
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. not divisible by 3

Explanation: Sum of digits of 145 is 10. A number is divisible by 3 when the digit sum is divisible by 3. Therefore, 145 is not divisible by 3.

Shortcut: For 3 and 9, check digit sum instead of division.

Q14. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=7; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

Recursion dry runarray/string logicMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 28
  2. B. 49
  3. C. 27
  4. D. 29
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 28

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 7. Sum = 7×(7+1)/2 = 28. Output is 28.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

Q15. What is the value of 5 % 3 in C-style programming?

Recursion basicsoutput predictionHardConcept Practice
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 2
  3. C. 15
  4. D. 8
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 2

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 5 divided by 3 leaves remainder 2.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q16. What is the unit digit of 7^60?

Base conditionunit digitEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 7
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 5
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 1

Explanation: Unit digits follow cycles. For base 7, calculate powers modulo 10. Since 60 is reduced within the unit-digit cycle, the unit digit is 1.

Shortcut: Use the unit digit cycle; never expand the full power.

Q17. Find the remainder when (12 × 55 + 21) is divided by 8.

Recursive callsremainderMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 3
  2. B. 1
  3. C. 2
  4. D. 4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 1

Explanation: First calculate 12 × 55 + 21 = 681. Divide by 8. The remainder is 1.

Shortcut: For remainder questions, reduce each term by the divisor before multiplying.

Q18. What does a factorial function return for input 8?

Factorialloop traceMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 40328
  2. B. 40320
  3. C. 5040
  4. D. 40319
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 40320

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 8. 8! = 40320.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

Q19. Classify the number 69.

Fibonacciplace valueHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Even
  2. B. Odd only
  3. C. Co-prime only
  4. D. Composite
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Composite

Explanation: Check divisibility up to √69. Since a divisor is found, 69 is composite.

Shortcut: To test prime, check only divisors up to square root.

Q20. In the number 8277, what is the place value of the digit 2?

Sum of digitsdivisibilityEasyConcept Practice
  1. A. 2000
  2. B. 2
  3. C. 20
  4. D. 200
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 200

Explanation: Place value means digit multiplied by its position value. In 8277, digit 2 is at the 100 place, so place value = 2 × 100 = 200.

Shortcut: Read from right: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands. Multiply the digit by its place.

Q21. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=4; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

Recursion dry rundebuggingMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 10
  2. B. 16
  3. C. 9
  4. D. 11
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 10

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 4. Sum = 4×(4+1)/2 = 10. Output is 10.

  3. C. 2
  4. D. 1

Q31. What is the unit digit of 14^75?

Recursive callsunit digitHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 6
  2. B. 8
  3. C. 0
  4. D. 4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 4

Explanation: Unit digits follow cycles. For base 14, calculate powers modulo 10. Since 75 is reduced within the unit-digit cycle, the unit digit is 4.

Shortcut: Use the unit digit cycle; never expand the full power.

Q32. What does a factorial function return for input 7?

Factorialdry runEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 5040
  2. B. 720
  3. C. 5047
  4. D. 5039
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 5040

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 7. 7! = 5040.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

Q33. Using divisibility rules, which statement is true for 165?

Fibonacciprime/compositeMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. multiple of 11
  2. B. prime number
  3. C. divisible by 9
  4. D. divisible by 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. divisible by 3

Explanation: Sum of digits of 165 is 12. A number is divisible by 3 when the digit sum is divisible by 3. Therefore, 165 is divisible by 3.

Shortcut: For 3 and 9, check digit sum instead of division.

Q34. Classify the number 84.

Sum of digitsplace valueMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Even
  2. B. Odd only
  3. C. Co-prime only
  4. D. Composite
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Composite

Explanation: Check divisibility up to √84. Since a divisor is found, 84 is composite.

Shortcut: To test prime, check only divisors up to square root.

Q35. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=3; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

Recursion dry runoutput predictionHardConcept Practice
  1. A. 9
  2. B. 5
  3. C. 6
  4. D. 7
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 6

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 3. Sum = 3×(3+1)/2 = 6. Output is 6.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

Q36. What is the value of 6 % 3 in C-style programming?

Recursion basicsdebuggingEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 0
  2. B. 9
  3. C. 18
  4. D. 2
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 0

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 6 divided by 3 leaves remainder 0.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q37. Find the remainder when (5 × 75 + 21) is divided by 7.

Base conditionremainderMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 0
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 6
  4. D. 5
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 4

Explanation: First calculate 5 × 75 + 21 = 396. Divide by 7. The remainder is 4.

Shortcut: For remainder questions, reduce each term by the divisor before multiplying.

Q38. Using divisibility rules, which statement is true for 170?

Recursive callsprime/compositeMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. divisible by 9
  2. B. not divisible by 3
  3. C. multiple of 11
  4. D. prime number
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. not divisible by 3

Explanation: Sum of digits of 170 is 8. A number is divisible by 3 when the digit sum is divisible by 3. Therefore, 170 is not divisible by 3.

Shortcut: For 3 and 9, check digit sum instead of division.

Q39. What does a factorial function return for input 9?

Factorialarray/string logicHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 362889
  2. B. 362879
  3. C. 40320
  4. D. 362880
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 362880

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 9. 9! = 362880.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

Q40. In the number 8457, what is the place value of the digit 4?

FibonaccidivisibilityEasyConcept Practice
  1. A. 4
  2. B. 400
  3. C. 4000
  4. D. 40
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 400

Explanation: Place value means digit multiplied by its position value. In 8457, digit 4 is at the 100 place, so place value = 4 × 100 = 400.

Shortcut: Read from right: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands. Multiply the digit by its place.

Q41. What is the unit digit of 8^85?

Sum of digitsunit digitMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 0
  2. B. 2
  3. C. 8
  4. D. 4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 8

Explanation: Unit digits follow cycles. For base 8, calculate powers modulo 10. Since 85 is reduced within the unit-digit cycle, the unit digit is 8.

Shortcut: Use the unit digit cycle; never expand the full power.

Q42. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=5; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

Recursion dry rundry runMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 14
  2. B. 16
  3. C. 15
  4. D. 25
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 15

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 5. Sum = 5×(5+1)/2 = 15. Output is 15.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

Q43. What is the value of 8 % 3 in C-style programming?

Recursion basicsloop traceHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 2
  2. B. 24
  3. C. 11
  4. D. 10
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 2

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 8 divided by 3 leaves remainder 2.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Base conditionplace valueEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Even
  2. B. Odd only
  3. C. Co-prime only
  4. D. Composite

Answer: D. Composite

Explanation: Check divisibility up to √94. Since a divisor is found, 94 is composite.

Recursive callsdivisibilityMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 50
  2. B. 5
  3. C. 500
  4. D. 5000
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 500

Explanation: Place value means digit multiplied by its position value. In 8502, digit 5 is at the 100 place, so place value = 5 × 100 = 500.

Shortcut: Read from right: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands. Multiply the digit by its place.

Q46. What does a factorial function return for input 6?

FactorialdebuggingMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 719
  2. B. 720
  3. C. 120
  4. D. 726
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 720

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 6. 6! = 720.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

Q47. Find the remainder when (6 × 85 + 21) is divided by 10.

FibonacciremainderHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 3
  2. B. 1
  3. C. 2
  4. D. 4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 1

Explanation: First calculate 6 × 85 + 21 = 531. Divide by 10. The remainder is 1.

Shortcut: For remainder questions, reduce each term by the divisor before multiplying.

Q48. Using divisibility rules, which statement is true for 180?

Sum of digitsprime/compositeEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. multiple of 11
  2. B. prime number
  3. C. divisible by 9
  4. D. divisible by 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. divisible by 3

Explanation: Sum of digits of 180 is 9. A number is divisible by 3 when the digit sum is divisible by 3. Therefore, 180 is divisible by 3.

Shortcut: For 3 and 9, check digit sum instead of division.

Q49. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=7; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

Recursion dry runarray/string logicMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 28
  2. B. 49
  3. C. 27
  4. D. 29
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 28

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 7. Sum = 7×(7+1)/2 = 28. Output is 28.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

Q50. What is the value of 5 % 3 in C-style programming?

Recursion basicsoutput predictionMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 2
  3. C. 15
  4. D. 8
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 2

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 5 divided by 3 leaves remainder 2.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q51. What is the unit digit of 10^95?

Base conditionunit digitHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 6
  2. B. 0
  3. C. 2
  4. D. 4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 0

Explanation: Unit digits follow cycles. For base 10, calculate powers modulo 10. Since 95 is reduced within the unit-digit cycle, the unit digit is 0.

Shortcut: Use the unit digit cycle; never expand the full power.

Q52. Find the remainder when (11 × 90 + 21) is divided by 8.

Recursive callsremainderEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 6
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 5
  4. D. 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 3

Explanation: First calculate 11 × 90 + 21 = 1011. Divide by 8. The remainder is 3.

Shortcut: For remainder questions, reduce each term by the divisor before multiplying.

End-of-Day Revision Card — Day 21

10-point summary

  1. Revise the definition and purpose of Recursion basics.
  2. Write formulas once by hand before attempting speed questions.
  3. Solve easy questions first to build accuracy.
  4. Use option elimination wherever full calculation is long.
  5. Track every mistake in the weak-area dashboard.
  6. Reattempt wrong quiz questions without seeing the answer.
  7. Mark completed topics only after solving examples.
  8. Keep verbal practice short but consistent.
  9. Use shortcuts only after understanding the concept.
  10. End the day by reviewing tomorrow’s topic list.

Formula recap

  • Loop dry run: Track variable after each iteration — Use a small table.
  • Modulo: a % b = remainder — Useful for digit and divisibility code.
  • Array traversal: Index 0 to n-1 — Avoid out-of-bound errors.
  • Divisibility by 2: Last digit is even — Use for quick even/odd and factor checks.
  • Divisibility by 3: Sum of digits divisible by 3 — Use before doing long division.

Tricks recap

  • Recursion basics: Before solving Recursion basics, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • Base condition: Before solving Base condition, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • Recursive calls: Before solving Recursive calls, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • Factorial: Before solving Factorial, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Mistake checklist

  • Wrong base value
  • Wrong unit conversion
  • Misread option
  • Skipped condition
  • No revision of incorrect answers

Weak-area revision advice: Any topic below 60% must be revised before moving to hard questions.

Basic math coding

Basic math coding builds programming logic for TCS NQT coding and MCQ rounds. Focus on dry runs, variable updates, loop boundaries, array indexing, string traversal, and base conditions in recursion.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Basic math coding.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Basic math coding, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Basic math coding; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember Dry run trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Basic math coding.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Basic math coding is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Dry run trick, Boundary check trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: for i=1 to 3, add i to sum.
sum starts 0. After i=1 sum=1, i=2 sum=3, i=3 sum=6. Output 6.
Shortcut: Make trace table.
Example 2: Check if 'madam' is palindrome.
Reverse is 'madam', same as original, so palindrome.
Shortcut: Compare both ends.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Basic math coding without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.
Prime number

Prime number is the foundation of quantitative aptitude. Start by understanding what a number represents, how digits contribute through place value, and how divisibility or remainder rules reduce calculation. In TCS NQT, these questions are usually short but accuracy matters. Solve by checking the structure of the number before doing long calculation.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Prime number.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Prime number, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Prime number; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember Last digit trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Prime number.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Prime number is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Last digit trick, Unit digit trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: Find whether 729 is divisible by 9.
Digit sum = 7+2+9 = 18. Since 18 is divisible by 9, 729 is divisible by 9.
Shortcut: Digit sum rule avoids division.
Example 2: Find LCM of 12 and 18.
HCF is 6. LCM = 12×18/6 = 36.
Shortcut: Use product/HCF.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Prime number without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.

LCM

LCM helps solve questions involving repetition, common multiples, common factors, factorial powers, and schedules. LCM answers 'when will things meet together again', while HCF answers 'largest common division possible'. Learn to identify whether the question asks for a common multiple or common factor.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to LCM.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving LCM, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for LCM; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember LCM schedule trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in LCM.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in LCM is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use LCM schedule trick, HCF division trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: Find whether 729 is divisible by 9.
Digit sum = 7+2+9 = 18. Since 18 is divisible by 9, 729 is divisible by 9.
Shortcut: Digit sum rule avoids division.
Example 2: Find LCM of 12 and 18.
HCF is 6. LCM = 12×18/6 = 36.
Shortcut: Use product/HCF.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting LCM without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.
Armstrong number

Armstrong number builds programming logic for TCS NQT coding and MCQ rounds. Focus on dry runs, variable updates, loop boundaries, array indexing, string traversal, and base conditions in recursion.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Armstrong number.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Armstrong number, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Armstrong number; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember Dry run trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Armstrong number.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Armstrong number is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Dry run trick, Boundary check trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: for i=1 to 3, add i to sum.
sum starts 0. After i=1 sum=1, i=2 sum=3, i=3 sum=6. Output 6.
Shortcut: Make trace table.
Example 2: Check if 'madam' is palindrome.
Reverse is 'madam', same as original, so palindrome.
Shortcut: Compare both ends.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Armstrong number without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.
Perfect number

Perfect number builds programming logic for TCS NQT coding and MCQ rounds. Focus on dry runs, variable updates, loop boundaries, array indexing, string traversal, and base conditions in recursion.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Perfect number.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Pattern recognition trick

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Dry run trick, Boundary check trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: for i=1 to 3, add i to sum.
sum starts 0. After i=1 sum=1, i=2 sum=3, i=3 sum=6. Output 6.
Shortcut: Make trace table.
Example 2: Check if 'madam' is palindrome.
Reverse is 'madam', same as original, so palindrome.
Shortcut: Compare both ends.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Perfect number without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.

Digit-based problems

Digit-based problems builds programming logic for TCS NQT coding and MCQ rounds. Focus on dry runs, variable updates, loop boundaries, array indexing, string traversal, and base conditions in recursion.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Digit-based problems.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Digit-based problems, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Digit-based problems; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember Dry run trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Digit-based problems.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Digit-based problems is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Dry run trick, Boundary check trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: for i=1 to 3, add i to sum.
sum starts 0. After i=1 sum=1, i=2 sum=3, i=3 sum=6. Output 6.
Shortcut: Make trace table.
Example 2: Check if 'madam' is palindrome.
Reverse is 'madam', same as original, so palindrome.
Shortcut: Compare both ends.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Digit-based problems without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.

Important Formulas and Rules

Formula/RuleExpressionWhen to use
Loop dry runTrack variable after each iterationUse a small table.
Moduloa % b = remainderUseful for digit and divisibility code.
Array traversalIndex 0 to n-1Avoid out-of-bound errors.
Divisibility by 2Last digit is evenUse for quick even/odd and factor checks.
Divisibility by 3Sum of digits divisible by 3Use before doing long division.
Divisibility by 9Sum of digits divisible by 9Useful for remainders and shortcuts.
LCM × HCFFor two numbers: LCM × HCF = productUse when HCF or LCM is known.
Highest power in n!⌊n/p⌋ + ⌊n/p²⌋ + ...Use for factorial prime powers.
Co-primeHCF = 1Numbers need not both be prime.

Practice Question Bank — 65 Questions

All questions below are original TCS NQT PYQ-style or concept-practice questions with answers and explanations.

Q1. What is the value of 6 % 4 in C-style programming?

Basic math codingdebuggingMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 2
  2. B. 24
  3. C. 1
  4. D. 10
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 2

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 6 divided by 4 leaves remainder 2.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q2. Find the remainder when (6 × 41 + 22) is divided by 7.

Prime numberremainderMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 5
  2. B. 2
  3. C. 4
  4. D. 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 2

Explanation: First calculate 6 × 41 + 22 = 268. Divide by 7. The remainder is 2.

Shortcut: For remainder questions, reduce each term by the divisor before multiplying.

Q3. Classify the string 'tcs' after comparing it with its reverse.

Factorialloop traceHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Not palindrome
  2. B. Anagram
  3. C. Empty string
  4. D. Sorted string
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Not palindrome

Explanation: Reverse of 'tcs' is 'sct'. Since it does not match, it is not palindrome.

Shortcut: Palindrome check: compare first with last, second with second last.

Q4. What does a factorial function return for input 9?

GCDarray/string logicEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 362889
  2. B. 362879
  3. C. 40320
  4. D. 362880
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 362880

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 9. 9! = 362880.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

Q5. Find the LCM of 22 and 25.

LCMLCMMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 551
  3. C. 47
  4. D. 550
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 550

Explanation: HCF(22,25) = 1. LCM × HCF = product of numbers. LCM = 22 × 25 ÷ 1 = 550.

Shortcut: Use LCM × HCF = product for two numbers.

Q6. A loop counts even numbers in [2, 3, 4, 5, 6]. What count is printed?

Armstrong numberdebuggingMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 20
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 2
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 3

Explanation: Even numbers are 2, 4 and 6, so the count is 3.

Shortcut: For count problems, increase counter only when condition is true.

Q7. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=5; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

Perfect numberdry runHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 14
  2. B. 16
  3. C. 15
  4. D. 25
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 15

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 5. Sum = 5×(5+1)/2 = 15. Output is 15.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

Q8. What is the value of 8 % 4 in C-style programming?

Digit-based problemsloop traceEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 32
  2. B. 12
  3. C. 0
  4. D. 2
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 0

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 8 divided by 4 leaves remainder 0.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q9. Given array [3, 4, 9, 4], what value will a maximum-finding loop return?

Basic math codingarray/string logicMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 3
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 20
  4. D. 9
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 9

Explanation: Compare each element and keep the largest. The maximum among [3, 4, 9, 4] is 9.

Shortcut: Initialize max with first element, then compare one by one.

Q10. In the number 8224, what is the place value of the digit 2?

Prime numberdivisibilityMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 2000
  2. B. 2
  3. C. 20
  4. D. 200
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 200

Explanation: Place value means digit multiplied by its position value. In 8224, digit 2 is at the 100 place, so place value = 2 × 100 = 200.

Shortcut: Read from right: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands. Multiply the digit by its place.

Q11. What does a factorial function return for input 6?

FactorialdebuggingHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 719
  2. B. 720
  3. C. 120
  4. D. 726
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 720

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 6. 6! = 720.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

Q12. What is the time complexity of a single loop that visits each element of an array once?

GCDdry runEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. O(n²)
  2. B. O(log n)
  3. C. O(1)
  4. D. O(n)
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. O(n)

Explanation: A single loop over n elements performs work proportional to n. Therefore time complexity is O(n).

Shortcut: One full traversal usually means O(n).

Q13. Numbers 28 and 44 are:

LCMco-primeMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Both prime
  2. B. LCM is 1
  3. C. Both even
  4. D. Not co-prime
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Not co-prime

Explanation: Two numbers are co-prime if their HCF is 1. HCF(28,44) = 4, so they are not co-prime.

Shortcut: Co-prime means common factor only 1; numbers need not both be prime.

Q14. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=7; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

Armstrong numberarray/string logicMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 28
  2. B. 49
  3. C. 27
  4. D. 29
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 28

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 7. Sum = 7×(7+1)/2 = 28. Output is 28.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

Q15. What is the value of 5 % 4 in C-style programming?

Perfect numberoutput predictionHardConcept Practice
  1. A. 9
  2. B. 20
  3. C. 17
  4. D. 1
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 1

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 5 divided by 4 leaves remainder 1.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q16. Given array [3, 4, 9, 3], what value will a maximum-finding loop return?

Digit-based problemsdebuggingEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 9
  2. B. 3
  3. C. 4
  4. D. 19
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 9

Explanation: Compare each element and keep the largest. The maximum among [3, 4, 9, 3] is 9.

Shortcut: Initialize max with first element, then compare one by one.

Q17. Classify the string 'tcs' after comparing it with its reverse.

Basic math codingdry runMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Not palindrome
  2. B. Anagram
  3. C. Empty string
  4. D. Sorted string
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Not palindrome

Explanation: Reverse of 'tcs' is 'sct'. Since it does not match, it is not palindrome.

Shortcut: Palindrome check: compare first with last, second with second last.

Q18. Using divisibility rules, which statement is true for 152?

Prime numberprime/compositeMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. divisible by 9
  2. B. not divisible by 3
  3. C. multiple of 11
  4. D. prime number
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. not divisible by 3

Explanation: Sum of digits of 152 is 8. A number is divisible by 3 when the digit sum is divisible by 3. Therefore, 152 is not divisible by 3.

Shortcut: For 3 and 9, check digit sum instead of division.

Q19. What is the time complexity of a single loop that visits each element of an array once?

Factorialarray/string logicHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. O(n²)
  2. B. O(log n)
  3. C. O(1)
  4. D. O(n)
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. O(n)

Explanation: A single loop over n elements performs work proportional to n. Therefore time complexity is O(n).

Shortcut: One full traversal usually means O(n).

Q20. A loop counts even numbers in [2, 3, 4, 5, 6]. What count is printed?

GCDoutput predictionEasyConcept Practice
  1. A. 20
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 2
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 3

Explanation: Even numbers are 2, 4 and 6, so the count is 3.

Shortcut: For count problems, increase counter only when condition is true.

Q21. Find the HCF of 69 and 97.

LCMHCFMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 9
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 7
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 1

Explanation: Divide both numbers by common factors or use Euclid's method. The greatest common divisor of 69 and 97 is 1.

Shortcut: Use Euclid: repeatedly replace larger number by remainder.

Q22. What is the value of 7 % 4 in C-style programming?

Armstrong numberdry runMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 28
  3. C. 11
  4. D. 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 3

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 7 divided by 4 leaves remainder 3.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q23. Given array [3, 4, 9, 6], what value will a maximum-finding loop return?

Perfect numberloop traceHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 4
  2. B. 22
  3. C. 9
  4. D. 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 9

Explanation: Compare each element and keep the largest. The maximum among [3, 4, 9, 6] is 9.

Shortcut: Initialize max with first element, then compare one by one.

Q24. Classify the string 'level' after comparing it with its reverse.

Digit-based problemsarray/string logicEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Palindrome
  2. B. Empty string
  3. C. Anagram
  4. D. Sorted string
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Palindrome

Explanation: Reverse of 'level' is 'level'. Since it matches, it is palindrome.

Shortcut: Palindrome check: compare first with last, second with second last.

Q25. What does a factorial function return for input 5?

Basic math codingoutput predictionMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 119
  2. B. 24
  3. C. 125
  4. D. 120
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 120

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 5. 5! = 120.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

Q26. What is the unit digit of 9^71?

Prime numberunit digitMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 5
  2. B. 3
  3. C. 1
  4. D. 9
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 9

Explanation: Unit digits follow cycles. For base 9, calculate powers modulo 10. Since 71 is reduced within the unit-digit cycle, the unit digit is 9.

Shortcut: Use the unit digit cycle; never expand the full power.

Q27. A loop counts even numbers in [2, 3, 4, 5, 6]. What count is printed?

Factorialdry runHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 20
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 2
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 3

Explanation: Even numbers are 2, 4 and 6, so the count is 3.

Shortcut: For count problems, increase counter only when condition is true.

Q28. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=6; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

GCDloop traceEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 22
  2. B. 20
  3. C. 21
  4. D. 36
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 21

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 6. Sum = 6×(6+1)/2 = 21. Output is 21.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

Q29. Two bells ring every 8 minutes and 8 minutes. If they ring together now, after how many minutes will they ring together again?

LCMfactorial powerMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 16
  2. B. 8
  3. C. 24
  4. D. 0
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 8

Explanation: Events repeating together use LCM. LCM(8,8) = 8, so bells meet again after 8 minutes.

Shortcut: Whenever schedules repeat together, use LCM.

Q30. Given array [3, 4, 9, 5], what value will a maximum-finding loop return?

Armstrong numberoutput predictionMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 3
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 21
  4. D. 9
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 9

Explanation: Compare each element and keep the largest. The maximum among [3, 4, 9, 5] is 9.

Shortcut: Initialize max with first element, then compare one by one.

Perfect numberdebuggingHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Not palindrome
  2. B. Anagram

  4. D. Sorted string
Show answer and explanation

Q32. What does a factorial function return for input 7?

Digit-based problemsdry runEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 5040
  2. B. 720
  3. C. 5047
  4. D. 5039
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 5040

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 7. 7! = 5040.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

Q33. What is the time complexity of a single loop that visits each element of an array once?

Basic math codingloop traceMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. O(n²)
  2. B. O(log n)
  3. C. O(1)
  4. D. O(n)
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. O(n)

Explanation: A single loop over n elements performs work proportional to n. Therefore time complexity is O(n).

Shortcut: One full traversal usually means O(n).

Q34. Classify the number 85.

Prime numberplace valueMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Even
  2. B. Odd only
  3. C. Co-prime only
  4. D. Composite
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Composite

Explanation: Check divisibility up to √85. Since a divisor is found, 85 is composite.

Shortcut: To test prime, check only divisors up to square root.

Q35. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=3; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

Factorialoutput predictionHardConcept Practice
  1. A. 9
  2. B. 5
  3. C. 6
  4. D. 7
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 6

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 3. Sum = 3×(3+1)/2 = 6. Output is 6.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

Q36. What is the value of 6 % 4 in C-style programming?

GCDdebuggingEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 2
  2. B. 24
  3. C. 1
  4. D. 10
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 2

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 6 divided by 4 leaves remainder 2.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q37. What is the highest power of 3 in 7! ?

LCMfactorsMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 2
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 2

Explanation: Use Legendre formula: floor(7/3) + floor(7/9) + ... = 2. Therefore highest power is 2.

Shortcut: For factorial powers, keep dividing n by p, p², p³.

Q42. Find the remainder when (10 × 81 + 22) is divided by 5.

Prime numberremainderMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 2
  2. B. 0
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 2

Explanation: First calculate 10 × 81 + 22 = 832. Divide by 5. The remainder is 2.

Shortcut: For remainder questions, reduce each term by the divisor before multiplying.

Q43. What is the value of 8 % 4 in C-style programming?

Factorialloop traceHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 32
  2. B. 12
  3. C. 0
  4. D. 2
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 0

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 8 divided by 4 leaves remainder 0.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q44. Given array [3, 4, 9, 3], what value will a maximum-finding loop return?

GCDarray/string logicEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 9
  2. B. 3
  3. C. 4
  4. D. 19
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 9

Explanation: Compare each element and keep the largest. The maximum among [3, 4, 9, 3] is 9.

Shortcut: Initialize max with first element, then compare one by one.

Q45. Find the LCM of 12 and 25.

LCMLCMMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 300
  3. C. 37
  4. D. 301
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 300

Explanation: HCF(12,25) = 1. LCM × HCF = product of numbers. LCM = 12 × 25 ÷ 1 = 300.

Shortcut: Use LCM × HCF = product for two numbers.

Q46. What does a factorial function return for input 6?

Armstrong numberdebuggingMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 719
  2. B. 720
  3. C. 120
  4. D. 726
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 720

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 6. 6! = 720.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

Q47. What is the time complexity of a single loop that visits each element of an array once?

Perfect numberdry runHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. O(n²)
  2. B. O(log n)
  3. C. O(1)
  4. D. O(n)
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. O(n)

Explanation: A single loop over n elements performs work proportional to n. Therefore time complexity is O(n).

Shortcut: One full traversal usually means O(n).

Q48. A loop counts even numbers in [2, 3, 4, 5, 6]. What count is printed?

Digit-based problemsloop traceEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 20
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 2
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 3

Explanation: Even numbers are 2, 4 and 6, so the count is 3.

Shortcut: For count problems, increase counter only when condition is true.

Q49. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=7; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

Basic math codingarray/string logicMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 28
  2. B. 49
  3. C. 27
  4. D. 29
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 28

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 7. Sum = 7×(7+1)/2 = 28. Output is 28.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

Q50. In the number 8584, what is the place value of the digit 5?

Prime numberdivisibilityMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 50
  2. B. 5
  3. C. 500
  4. D. 5000
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 500

Explanation: Place value means digit multiplied by its position value. In 8584, digit 5 is at the 100 place, so place value = 5 × 100 = 500.

Shortcut: Read from right: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands. Multiply the digit by its place.

Q51. Given array [3, 4, 9, 6], what value will a maximum-finding loop return?

FactorialdebuggingHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 4
  2. B. 22
  3. C. 9
  4. D. 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 9

Explanation: Compare each element and keep the largest. The maximum among [3, 4, 9, 6] is 9.

Shortcut: Initialize max with first element, then compare one by one.

Q52. Classify the string 'level' after comparing it with its reverse.

GCDdry runEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Palindrome
  2. B. Empty string
  3. C. Anagram
  4. D. Sorted string
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Palindrome

Explanation: Reverse of 'level' is 'level'. Since it matches, it is palindrome.

Shortcut: Palindrome check: compare first with last, second with second last.

Q53. Numbers 68 and 42 are:

LCMco-primeMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Both prime
  2. B. LCM is 1
  3. C. Both even
  4. D. Not co-prime
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Not co-prime

Explanation: Two numbers are co-prime if their HCF is 1. HCF(68,42) = 2, so they are not co-prime.

Shortcut: Co-prime means common factor only 1; numbers need not both be prime.

Q54. What is the time complexity of a single loop that visits each element of an array once?

Armstrong numberarray/string logicMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. O(n²)
  2. B. O(log n)
  3. C. O(1)
  4. D. O(n)
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. O(n)

Explanation: A single loop over n elements performs work proportional to n. Therefore time complexity is O(n).

Shortcut: One full traversal usually means O(n).

Q55. A loop counts even numbers in [2, 3, 4, 5, 6]. What count is printed?

Perfect numberoutput predictionHardConcept Practice
  1. A. 20
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 2
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 3

Explanation: Even numbers are 2, 4 and 6, so the count is 3.

Shortcut: For count problems, increase counter only when condition is true.

Q56. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=4; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

Digit-based problemsdebuggingEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 10
  2. B. 16
  3. C. 9
  4. D. 11
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 10

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 4. Sum = 4×(4+1)/2 = 10. Output is 10.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

Q57. What is the value of 7 % 4 in C-style programming?

Basic math codingdry runMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 28
  3. C. 11
  4. D. 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 3

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 7 divided by 4 leaves remainder 3.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q58. Using divisibility rules, which statement is true for 192?

Prime numberprime/compositeMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. multiple of 11
  2. B. prime number
  3. C. divisible by 9
  4. D. divisible by 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. divisible by 3

Explanation: Sum of digits of 192 is 12. A number is divisible by 3 when the digit sum is divisible by 3. Therefore, 192 is divisible by 3.

Shortcut: For 3 and 9, check digit sum instead of division.

Q59. Classify the string 'tcs' after comparing it with its reverse.

Factorialarray/string logicHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Not palindrome
  2. B. Anagram
  3. C. Empty string
  4. D. Sorted string
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Not palindrome

Explanation: Reverse of 'tcs' is 'sct'. Since it does not match, it is not palindrome.

Shortcut: Palindrome check: compare first with last, second with second last.

Q60. What does a factorial function return for input 5?

GCDoutput predictionEasyConcept Practice
  1. A. 119
  2. B. 24
  3. C. 125
  4. D. 120
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 120

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 5. 5! = 120.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

Q61. Find the HCF of 109 and 95.

LCMHCFMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 9
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 7
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 1

Explanation: Divide both numbers by common factors or use Euclid's method. The greatest common divisor of 109 and 95 is 1.

Shortcut: Use Euclid: repeatedly replace larger number by remainder.

Q62. A loop counts even numbers in [2, 3, 4, 5, 6]. What count is printed?

Armstrong numberdry runMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 20
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 2
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 3

Explanation: Even numbers are 2, 4 and 6, so the count is 3.

Shortcut: For count problems, increase counter only when condition is true.

Q63. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=6; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

Perfect numberloop traceHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 22
  2. B. 20
  3. C. 21
  4. D. 36
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 21

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 6. Sum = 6×(6+1)/2 = 21. Output is 21.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

Q64. What is the value of 9 % 4 in C-style programming?

Digit-based problemsarray/string logicEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 2
  2. B. 36
  3. C. 1
  4. D. 13
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 1

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 9 divided by 4 leaves remainder 1.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q65. Given array [3, 4, 9, 4], what value will a maximum-finding loop return?

Basic math codingoutput predictionMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 3
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 20
  4. D. 9
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 9

Explanation: Compare each element and keep the largest. The maximum among [3, 4, 9, 4] is 9.

Shortcut: Initialize max with first element, then compare one by one.

End-of-Day Revision Card — Day 22

10-point summary

  1. Revise the definition and purpose of Basic math coding.
  2. Write formulas once by hand before attempting speed questions.
  3. Solve easy questions first to build accuracy.
  4. Use option elimination wherever full calculation is long.
  5. Track every mistake in the weak-area dashboard.
  6. Reattempt wrong quiz questions without seeing the answer.
  7. Mark completed topics only after solving examples.
  8. Keep verbal practice short but consistent.
  9. Use shortcuts only after understanding the concept.
  10. End the day by reviewing tomorrow’s topic list.

Formula recap

  • Loop dry run: Track variable after each iteration — Use a small table.
  • Modulo: a % b = remainder — Useful for digit and divisibility code.
  • Array traversal: Index 0 to n-1 — Avoid out-of-bound errors.
  • Divisibility by 2: Last digit is even — Use for quick even/odd and factor checks.
  • Divisibility by 3: Sum of digits divisible by 3 — Use before doing long division.

Tricks recap

  • Basic math coding: Before solving Basic math coding, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • Prime number: Before solving Prime number, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • Factorial: Before solving Factorial, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • GCD: Before solving GCD, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Mistake checklist

  • Wrong base value
  • Wrong unit conversion
  • Misread option
  • Skipped condition
  • No revision of incorrect answers

Weak-area revision advice: Any topic below 60% must be revised before moving to hard questions.

Mixed programming logic

Mixed programming logic builds programming logic for TCS NQT coding and MCQ rounds. Focus on dry runs, variable updates, loop boundaries, array indexing, string traversal, and base conditions in recursion.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Mixed programming logic.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Mixed programming logic, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Mixed programming logic; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember Dry run trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Mixed programming logic.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Mixed programming logic is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Dry run trick, Boundary check trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: for i=1 to 3, add i to sum.
sum starts 0. After i=1 sum=1, i=2 sum=3, i=3 sum=6. Output 6.
Shortcut: Make trace table.
Example 2: Check if 'madam' is palindrome.
Reverse is 'madam', same as original, so palindrome.
Shortcut: Compare both ends.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Mixed programming logic without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.

Practice Question Bank — 65 Questions

All questions below are original TCS NQT PYQ-style or concept-practice questions with answers and explanations.

Q1. What is the value of 6 % 5 in C-style programming?

Mixed programming logicdebuggingMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 11
  2. B. 30
  3. C. 9
  4. D. 1
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 1

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 6 divided by 5 leaves remainder 1.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q2. Given array [4, 4, 9, 5], what value will a maximum-finding loop return?

Coding mock practicedry runMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 9
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 22
  4. D. 17
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 9

Explanation: Compare each element and keep the largest. The maximum among [4, 4, 9, 5] is 9.

Shortcut: Initialize max with first element, then compare one by one.

Q3. Classify the string 'tcs' after comparing it with its reverse.

Array + string mixed problemsloop traceHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Not palindrome
  2. B. Anagram
  3. C. Empty string
  4. D. Sorted string
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Not palindrome

Explanation: Reverse of 'tcs' is 'sct'. Since it does not match, it is not palindrome.

Shortcut: Palindrome check: compare first with last, second with second last.

Q4. What does a factorial function return for input 9?

Pattern printingarray/string logicEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 362889
  2. B. 362879
  3. C. 40320
  4. D. 362880
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 362880

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 9. 9! = 362880.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

Q5. What is the time complexity of a single loop that visits each element of an array once?

Debugging MCQsoutput predictionMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. O(n²)
  2. B. O(log n)
  3. C. O(1)
  4. D. O(n)
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. O(n)

Explanation: A single loop over n elements performs work proportional to n. Therefore time complexity is O(n).

Shortcut: One full traversal usually means O(n).

  4. D. 2

Q7. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=5; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

  1. A. 14
  2. B. 16
  3. C. 15
  4. D. 25
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 15

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 5. Sum = 5×(5+1)/2 = 15. Output is 15.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

  1. A. 1
  2. B. 13
  3. C. 40
  4. D. 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 3

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q11. What does a factorial function return for input 6?

Debugging MCQsdebuggingHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 719
  2. B. 720
  3. C. 120
  4. D. 726
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 720

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 6. 6! = 720.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

Q12. What is the time complexity of a single loop that visits each element of an array once?

Week 3 coding revisiondry runEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. O(n²)
  2. B. O(log n)
  3. C. O(1)
  4. D. O(n)
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. O(n)

Explanation: A single loop over n elements performs work proportional to n. Therefore time complexity is O(n).

Shortcut: One full traversal usually means O(n).

Q13. A loop counts even numbers in [2, 3, 4, 5, 6]. What count is printed?

Mixed programming logicloop traceMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 20
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 2
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 3

Explanation: Even numbers are 2, 4 and 6, so the count is 3.

Shortcut: For count problems, increase counter only when condition is true.

Q14. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=7; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

Coding mock practicearray/string logicMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 28
  2. B. 49
  3. C. 27
  4. D. 29
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 28

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 7. Sum = 7×(7+1)/2 = 28. Output is 28.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

Q15. What is the value of 5 % 5 in C-style programming?

Array + string mixed problemsoutput predictionHardConcept Practice
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 0
  3. C. 10
  4. D. 25
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 0

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 5 divided by 5 leaves remainder 0.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q16. Given array [4, 4, 9, 3], what value will a maximum-finding loop return?

Pattern printingdebuggingEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 3
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 20
  4. D. 9
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 9

Explanation: Compare each element and keep the largest. The maximum among [4, 4, 9, 3] is 9.

Shortcut: Initialize max with first element, then compare one by one.

Q17. Classify the string 'tcs' after comparing it with its reverse.

Debugging MCQsdry runMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Not palindrome
  2. B. Anagram
  3. C. Empty string
  4. D. Sorted string
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Not palindrome

Explanation: Reverse of 'tcs' is 'sct'. Since it does not match, it is not palindrome.

Shortcut: Palindrome check: compare first with last, second with second last.

Week 3 coding revisionloop traceMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 40328
  2. B. 40320
  3. C. 5040
  4. D. 40319
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 40320

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 8. 8! = 40320.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=4; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

Array + string mixed problemsdebuggingMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 10
  2. B. 16
  3. C. 9
  4. D. 11
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 10

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 4. Sum = 4×(4+1)/2 = 10. Output is 10.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

Pattern printingdry runMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 12
  2. B. 1
  3. C. 2
  4. D. 35
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 2

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 7 divided by 5 leaves remainder 2.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

  2. B. 9
  3. C. 17
  4. D. 23

Answer: B. 9

Day 23 Score Analyzer

Total Attempted: 0
Correct: 0

End-of-Day Revision Card — Day 23

10-point summary

  1. Revise the definition and purpose of Mixed programming logic.
  2. Write formulas once by hand before attempting speed questions.
  3. Solve easy questions first to build accuracy.
  4. Use option elimination wherever full calculation is long.
  5. Track every mistake in the weak-area dashboard.
  6. Reattempt wrong quiz questions without seeing the answer.
  7. Mark completed topics only after solving examples.
  8. Keep verbal practice short but consistent.
  9. Use shortcuts only after understanding the concept.
  10. End the day by reviewing tomorrow’s topic list.

Formula recap

  • Loop dry run: Track variable after each iteration — Use a small table.
  • Modulo: a % b = remainder — Useful for digit and divisibility code.
  • Array traversal: Index 0 to n-1 — Avoid out-of-bound errors.

Tricks recap

  • Mixed programming logic: Before solving Mixed programming logic, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • Coding mock practice: Before solving Coding mock practice, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • Array + string mixed problems: Before solving Array + string mixed problems, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • Pattern printing: Before solving Pattern printing, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Mistake checklist

  • Wrong base value
  • Wrong unit conversion
  • Misread option
  • Skipped condition
  • No revision of incorrect answers

Weak-area revision advice: Any topic below 60% must be revised before moving to hard questions.

Day 24 — Full Mock Test 1 + Time Management

Study objective: Build practical command over Full mock test 1, Foundation section practice, Advanced section practice, Coding section practice and apply it in TCS NQT-style timed questions.

Full mock test 1

Full mock test 1 is an important part of the TCS NQT preparation plan. Understand the definition, observe solved examples, then practise mixed questions.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Full mock test 1.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Full mock test 1, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Full mock test 1; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember Concept trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Full mock test 1.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Full mock test 1 is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Concept trick, Pattern recognition trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: Choose correct: 'He go' or 'He goes'.
'He goes' is correct because singular subject uses singular verb.
Shortcut: Check subject first.
Example 2: Synonym of rapid.
Rapid means fast or quick.
Shortcut: Use context clue.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Full mock test 1 without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.
Foundation section practice

Foundation section practice is the foundation of quantitative aptitude. Start by understanding what a number represents, how digits contribute through place value, and how divisibility or remainder rules reduce calculation. In TCS NQT, these questions are usually short but accuracy matters. Solve by checking the structure of the number before doing long calculation.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Foundation section practice.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Foundation section practice, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Foundation section practice; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember Last digit trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Foundation section practice.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Foundation section practice is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Last digit trick, Unit digit trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: Find whether 729 is divisible by 9.
Digit sum = 7+2+9 = 18. Since 18 is divisible by 9, 729 is divisible by 9.
Shortcut: Digit sum rule avoids division.
Example 2: Find LCM of 12 and 18.
HCF is 6. LCM = 12×18/6 = 36.
Shortcut: Use product/HCF.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Foundation section practice without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.
Advanced section practice

Advanced section practice is the foundation of quantitative aptitude. Start by understanding what a number represents, how digits contribute through place value, and how divisibility or remainder rules reduce calculation. In TCS NQT, these questions are usually short but accuracy matters. Solve by checking the structure of the number before doing long calculation.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Advanced section practice.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Last digit trick, Unit digit trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Digit sum = 7+2+9 = 18. Since 18 is divisible by 9, 729 is divisible by 9.
Shortcut: Digit sum rule avoids division.

HCF is 6. LCM = 12×18/6 = 36.
