30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use DST triangle trick, Unit conversion trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: Speed = 60 km/h, time = 2.5 h. Find distance.
Distance = 60×2.5 = 150 km.
Shortcut: Use D = S×T.
Example 2: Convert 72 km/h to m/s.
72×5/18 = 20 m/s.
Shortcut: Use ×5/18.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Grammar speed revision without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.

Important Formulas and Rules

Formula/RuleExpressionWhen to use
DistanceSpeed × TimeBasic time-speed-distance.
SpeedDistance / TimeUse common units.
TimeDistance / SpeedConvert hours/minutes where needed.
Divisibility by 2Last digit is evenUse for quick even/odd and factor checks.
Divisibility by 3Sum of digits divisible by 3Use before doing long division.
Divisibility by 9Sum of digits divisible by 9Useful for remainders and shortcuts.
AP nth terma + (n-1)dCommon difference.
AP sumn/2[2a+(n-1)d]Sum of AP terms.
GP nth terma×r^(n-1)Common ratio.

Practice Question Bank — 65 Questions

All questions below are original TCS NQT PYQ-style or concept-practice questions with answers and explanations.

Q1. A person travels 125 km at 45 km/h and 84 km at 35 km/h. Find the average speed.

Foundation speed drillaverage speedMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 45.36
  2. B. 40
  3. C. 40.36
  4. D. 35.36
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 40.36

Explanation: Average speed = total distance / total time = (125+84) / (125/45+84/35) = 40.36 km/h.

Shortcut: Never average speeds directly unless equal time is given.

Q2. Find the remainder when (6 × 45 + 26) is divided by 7.

1-minute question solvingremainderMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 5
  2. B. 2
  3. C. 4
  4. D. 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 2

Explanation: First calculate 6 × 45 + 26 = 296. Divide by 7. The remainder is 2.

Shortcut: For remainder questions, reduce each term by the divisor before multiplying.

Q3. What is the remainder when 103 is divided by 9?

Approximation tricksadvanced ratioHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 5
  2. B. 6
  3. C. 12
  4. D. 4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 4

Explanation: Remainder by 9 can be found using digit sum. Digit sum of 103 is 4 and remainder is 4.

Shortcut: For 9, repeatedly sum digits to get remainder.

Q4. Classify the number 59.

Option eliminationplace valueEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Odd only
  2. B. Co-prime only
  3. C. Even
  4. D. Prime
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Prime

Explanation: Check divisibility up to √59. Since no divisor is found, 59 is prime.

Shortcut: To test prime, check only divisors up to square root.

Q5. In a race, A gives B a lead of 125 m and gains at 2 m/s. After how many seconds will A catch B?

Reading comprehension speedraceMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 127
  2. B. 52.5
  3. C. 72.5
  4. D. 62.5
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 62.5

Explanation: Catch-up time = lead / relative speed = 125/2 = 62.5 seconds.

Shortcut: In races, catching time = gap divided by relative speed.

Q6. A car travels at 70 km/h for 4 hours. Find the distance.

Grammar speed revisiondistance-timeMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 260
  2. B. 280
  3. C. 74
  4. D. 300
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 280

Explanation: Distance = speed × time = 70 × 4 = 280 km.

Shortcut: DST triangle: Distance = Speed × Time.

Q7. A person travels 155 km at 45 km/h and 108 km at 35 km/h. Find the average speed.

Foundation speed drillaverage speedHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 40.27
  2. B. 35.27
  3. C. 45.27
  4. D. 40
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 40.27

Explanation: Average speed = total distance / total time = (155+108) / (155/45+108/35) = 40.27 km/h.

Shortcut: Never average speeds directly unless equal time is given.

Q8. Using divisibility rules, which statement is true for 150?

1-minute question solvingprime/compositeEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. multiple of 11
  2. B. prime number
  3. C. divisible by 9
  4. D. divisible by 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. divisible by 3

Explanation: Sum of digits of 150 is 6. A number is divisible by 3 when the digit sum is divisible by 3. Therefore, 150 is divisible by 3.

Shortcut: For 3 and 9, check digit sum instead of division.

Q9. Find the 5th term of GP with first term 2 and common ratio 4.

Approximation tricksmixed quantMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 128
  2. B. 516
  3. C. 2048
  4. D. 512
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 512

Explanation: GP nth term = a × r^(n-1). So 5th term = 2 × 4^4 = 512.

Shortcut: For GP, multiply by same ratio repeatedly.

Q10. In the number 8372, what is the place value of the digit 3?

Option eliminationdivisibilityMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 30
  2. B. 3
  3. C. 300
  4. D. 3000
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 300

Explanation: Place value means digit multiplied by its position value. In 8372, digit 3 is at the 100 place, so place value = 3 × 100 = 300.

Shortcut: Read from right: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands. Multiply the digit by its place.

Q11. In a race, A gives B a lead of 155 m and gains at 3 m/s. After how many seconds will A catch B?

Reading comprehension speedraceHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 158
  2. B. 51.67
  3. C. 41.67
  4. D. 61.67
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 51.67

Explanation: Catch-up time = lead / relative speed = 155/3 = 51.67 seconds.

Shortcut: In races, catching time = gap divided by relative speed.

Q12. A car travels at 60 km/h for 4 hours. Find the distance.

Grammar speed revisiondistance-timeEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 260
  2. B. 240
  3. C. 64
  4. D. 220
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 240

Explanation: Distance = speed × time = 60 × 4 = 240 km.

Shortcut: DST triangle: Distance = Speed × Time.

Q13. A person travels 185 km at 45 km/h and 132 km at 35 km/h. Find the average speed.

Foundation speed drillaverage speedMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 45.22
  2. B. 35.22
  3. C. 40.22
  4. D. 40
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 40.22

Explanation: Average speed = total distance / total time = (185+132) / (185/45+132/35) = 40.22 km/h.

Shortcut: Never average speeds directly unless equal time is given.

Q14. Classify the number 69.

1-minute question solvingplace valueMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Even
  2. B. Odd only
  3. C. Co-prime only
  4. D. Composite
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Composite

Explanation: Check divisibility up to √69. Since a divisor is found, 69 is composite.

Shortcut: To test prime, check only divisors up to square root.

Q15. What is the remainder when 115 is divided by 9?

Approximation tricksAPHardConcept Practice
  1. A. 8
  2. B. 2
  3. C. 0
  4. D. 7
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 7

Explanation: Remainder by 9 can be found using digit sum. Digit sum of 115 is 7 and remainder is 7.

Shortcut: For 9, repeatedly sum digits to get remainder.

Q16. What is the unit digit of 7^65?

Option eliminationunit digitEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 7
  2. B. 1
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 9
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 7

Explanation: Unit digits follow cycles. For base 7, calculate powers modulo 10. Since 65 is reduced within the unit-digit cycle, the unit digit is 7.

Shortcut: Use the unit digit cycle; never expand the full power.

Q17. In a race, A gives B a lead of 185 m and gains at 4 m/s. After how many seconds will A catch B?

Reading comprehension speedraceMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 46.25
  2. B. 56.25
  3. C. 189
  4. D. 36.25
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 46.25

Explanation: Catch-up time = lead / relative speed = 185/4 = 46.25 seconds.

Shortcut: In races, catching time = gap divided by relative speed.

Q18. A car travels at 50 km/h for 4 hours. Find the distance.

Grammar speed revisiondistance-timeMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 220
  2. B. 180
  3. C. 200
  4. D. 54
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 200

Explanation: Distance = speed × time = 50 × 4 = 200 km.

Shortcut: DST triangle: Distance = Speed × Time.

Q19. A person travels 215 km at 45 km/h and 156 km at 35 km/h. Find the average speed.

Foundation speed drillaverage speedHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 40
  2. B. 40.17
  3. C. 45.17
  4. D. 35.17
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 40.17

Explanation: Average speed = total distance / total time = (215+156) / (215/45+156/35) = 40.17 km/h.

Shortcut: Never average speeds directly unless equal time is given.

Q20. In the number 8462, what is the place value of the digit 4?

1-minute question solvingdivisibilityEasyConcept Practice
  1. A. 4
  2. B. 400
  3. C. 4000
  4. D. 40
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 400

Explanation: Place value means digit multiplied by its position value. In 8462, digit 4 is at the 100 place, so place value = 4 × 100 = 400.

Shortcut: Read from right: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands. Multiply the digit by its place.

Q21. Find the 5th term of GP with first term 2 and common ratio 4.

Approximation tricksGPMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 128
  2. B. 516
  3. C. 2048
  4. D. 512
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 512

Explanation: GP nth term = a × r^(n-1). So 5th term = 2 × 4^4 = 512.

Shortcut: For GP, multiply by same ratio repeatedly.

Q22. Find the remainder when (8 × 65 + 26) is divided by 6.

Option eliminationremainderMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 0
  2. B. 2
  3. C. 1
  4. D. 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 0

Explanation: First calculate 8 × 65 + 26 = 546. Divide by 6. The remainder is 0.

Shortcut: For remainder questions, reduce each term by the divisor before multiplying.

Q23. In a race, A gives B a lead of 215 m and gains at 5 m/s. After how many seconds will A catch B?

Reading comprehension speedraceHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 43
  2. B. 220
  3. C. 53
  4. D. 33
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 43

Explanation: Catch-up time = lead / relative speed = 215/5 = 43 seconds.

Shortcut: In races, catching time = gap divided by relative speed.

Q24. A car travels at 40 km/h for 4 hours. Find the distance.

Grammar speed revisiondistance-timeEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 160
  2. B. 180
  3. C. 44
  4. D. 140
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 160

Explanation: Distance = speed × time = 40 × 4 = 160 km.

Shortcut: DST triangle: Distance = Speed × Time.

Q25. A person travels 245 km at 45 km/h and 180 km at 35 km/h. Find the average speed.

Foundation speed drillaverage speedMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 45.14
  2. B. 40
  3. C. 40.14
  4. D. 35.14
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 40.14

Explanation: Average speed = total distance / total time = (245+180) / (245/45+180/35) = 40.14 km/h.

Shortcut: Never average speeds directly unless equal time is given.

Q26. What is the unit digit of 9^75?

1-minute question solvingunit digitMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 5
  2. B. 3
  3. C. 1
  4. D. 9
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 9

Explanation: Unit digits follow cycles. For base 9, calculate powers modulo 10. Since 75 is reduced within the unit-digit cycle, the unit digit is 9.

Shortcut: Use the unit digit cycle; never expand the full power.

Q27. What is the remainder when 127 is divided by 9?

Approximation tricksnumber theoryHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 2
  2. B. 1
  3. C. 8
  4. D. 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 1

Explanation: Remainder by 9 can be found using digit sum. Digit sum of 127 is 10 and remainder is 1.

Shortcut: For 9, repeatedly sum digits to get remainder.

Q28. Using divisibility rules, which statement is true for 170?

Option eliminationprime/compositeEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. divisible by 9
  2. B. not divisible by 3
  3. C. multiple of 11
  4. D. prime number
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. not divisible by 3

Explanation: Sum of digits of 170 is 8. A number is divisible by 3 when the digit sum is divisible by 3. Therefore, 170 is not divisible by 3.

Shortcut: For 3 and 9, check digit sum instead of division.

Q29. In a race, A gives B a lead of 245 m and gains at 6 m/s. After how many seconds will A catch B?

Reading comprehension speedraceMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 251
  2. B. 30.83
  3. C. 40.83
  4. D. 50.83
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 40.83

Explanation: Catch-up time = lead / relative speed = 245/6 = 40.83 seconds.

Shortcut: In races, catching time = gap divided by relative speed.

Q30. A car travels at 70 km/h for 4 hours. Find the distance.

Grammar speed revisiondistance-timeMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 260
  2. B. 280
  3. C. 74
  4. D. 300
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 280

Explanation: Distance = speed × time = 70 × 4 = 280 km.

Shortcut: DST triangle: Distance = Speed × Time.

  1. A. 35.12
  2. B. 45.12
  3. C. 40.12
  4. D. 40
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 40.12

Explanation: Average speed = total distance / total time = (275+204) / (275/45+204/35) = 40.12 km/h.

Shortcut: Never average speeds directly unless equal time is given.

Q32. Find the remainder when (9 × 75 + 26) is divided by 9.

1-minute question solvingremainderEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 0
  2. B. 2
  3. C. 1
  4. D. 8
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 8

Explanation: First calculate 9 × 75 + 26 = 701. Divide by 9. The remainder is 8.

Shortcut: For remainder questions, reduce each term by the divisor before multiplying.

Q33. Find the 5th term of GP with first term 2 and common ratio 4.

Approximation tricksadvanced ratioMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 128
  2. B. 516
  3. C. 2048
  4. D. 512
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 512

Explanation: GP nth term = a × r^(n-1). So 5th term = 2 × 4^4 = 512.

Shortcut: For GP, multiply by same ratio repeatedly.

Q34. Classify the number 89.

Option eliminationplace valueMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Odd only
  2. B. Co-prime only
  3. C. Even
  4. D. Prime
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Prime

Explanation: Check divisibility up to √89. Since no divisor is found, 89 is prime.

Shortcut: To test prime, check only divisors up to square root.

  1. A. 127.5
  2. B. 137.5
  3. C. 147.5
  4. D. 277
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 137.5

Explanation: Catch-up time = lead / relative speed = 275/2 = 137.5 seconds.

Shortcut: In races, catching time = gap divided by relative speed.

  1. A. 260

  4. D. 220

Q38. Using divisibility rules, which statement is true for 180?

1-minute question solvingprime/compositeMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. multiple of 11
  2. B. prime number
  3. C. divisible by 9
  4. D. divisible by 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. divisible by 3

Explanation: Sum of digits of 180 is 9. A number is divisible by 3 when the digit sum is divisible by 3. Therefore, 180 is divisible by 3.

Shortcut: For 3 and 9, check digit sum instead of division.

  4. D. 4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 4

End-of-Day Revision Card — Day 26

10-point summary

  1. Revise the definition and purpose of Foundation speed drill.
  2. Write formulas once by hand before attempting speed questions.
  3. Solve easy questions first to build accuracy.
  4. Use option elimination wherever full calculation is long.
  5. Track every mistake in the weak-area dashboard.
  6. Reattempt wrong quiz questions without seeing the answer.
  7. Mark completed topics only after solving examples.
  8. Keep verbal practice short but consistent.
  9. Use shortcuts only after understanding the concept.
  10. End the day by reviewing tomorrow’s topic list.

Formula recap

  • Distance: Speed × Time — Basic time-speed-distance.
  • Speed: Distance / Time — Use common units.
  • Time: Distance / Speed — Convert hours/minutes where needed.
  • Divisibility by 2: Last digit is even — Use for quick even/odd and factor checks.
  • Divisibility by 3: Sum of digits divisible by 3 — Use before doing long division.

Tricks recap

  • Foundation speed drill: Before solving Foundation speed drill, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • 1-minute question solving: Before solving 1-minute question solving, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • Approximation tricks: Before solving Approximation tricks, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • Option elimination: Before solving Option elimination, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Mistake checklist

  • Wrong base value
  • Wrong unit conversion
  • Misread option
  • Skipped condition
  • No revision of incorrect answers

Weak-area revision advice: Any topic below 60% must be revised before moving to hard questions.

Tomorrow’s preparation preview: Day 27 — Full Mock Test 2 + Coding Focus.

Day 27 • 22 June 2026

Day 27 — Full Mock Test 2 + Coding Focus

Study objective: Build practical command over Full mock test 2, Coding focus, One easy coding problem, One medium coding problem and apply it in TCS NQT-style timed questions.

Previous DayNext Day
Day mini TOC: Full mock test 2 Coding focus One easy coding problem One medium coding problem Programming logic MCQs Score improvement plan PracticeQuizScoreRevision

Topics covered

  • Full mock test 2
  • Coding focus
  • One easy coding problem
  • One medium coding problem
  • Programming logic MCQs
  • Score improvement plan
Full mock test 2

Full mock test 2 is an important part of the TCS NQT preparation plan. Understand the definition, observe solved examples, then practise mixed questions.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Full mock test 2.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Full mock test 2, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Full mock test 2; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember Concept trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Full mock test 2.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Full mock test 2 is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Concept trick, Pattern recognition trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: Choose correct: 'He go' or 'He goes'.
'He goes' is correct because singular subject uses singular verb.
Shortcut: Check subject first.
Example 2: Synonym of rapid.
Rapid means fast or quick.
Shortcut: Use context clue.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Full mock test 2 without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.
Coding focus

Coding focus builds programming logic for TCS NQT coding and MCQ rounds. Focus on dry runs, variable updates, loop boundaries, array indexing, string traversal, and base conditions in recursion.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Coding focus.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Coding focus, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Coding focus; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember Dry run trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Coding focus.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Coding focus is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Dry run trick, Boundary check trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: for i=1 to 3, add i to sum.
sum starts 0. After i=1 sum=1, i=2 sum=3, i=3 sum=6. Output 6.
Shortcut: Make trace table.
Example 2: Check if 'madam' is palindrome.
Reverse is 'madam', same as original, so palindrome.
Shortcut: Compare both ends.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Coding focus without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.

Programming logic MCQs

Score improvement plan

Score improvement plan is the foundation of quantitative aptitude. Start by understanding what a number represents, how digits contribute through place value, and how divisibility or remainder rules reduce calculation. In TCS NQT, these questions are usually short but accuracy matters. Solve by checking the structure of the number before doing long calculation.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Score improvement plan.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Score improvement plan, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Score improvement plan; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember Last digit trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Score improvement plan.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Score improvement plan is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Last digit trick, Unit digit trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: Find whether 729 is divisible by 9.
Digit sum = 7+2+9 = 18. Since 18 is divisible by 9, 729 is divisible by 9.
Shortcut: Digit sum rule avoids division.
Example 2: Find LCM of 12 and 18.
HCF is 6. LCM = 12×18/6 = 36.
Shortcut: Use product/HCF.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Score improvement plan without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.

Important Formulas and Rules

Formula/RuleExpressionWhen to use
Loop dry runTrack variable after each iterationUse a small table.
Moduloa % b = remainderUseful for digit and divisibility code.
Array traversalIndex 0 to n-1Avoid out-of-bound errors.
Divisibility by 2Last digit is evenUse for quick even/odd and factor checks.
Divisibility by 3Sum of digits divisible by 3Use before doing long division.
Divisibility by 9Sum of digits divisible by 9Useful for remainders and shortcuts.

Practice Question Bank — 65 Questions

All questions below are original TCS NQT PYQ-style or concept-practice questions with answers and explanations.

Q1. A value 105 is increased by 12%. What is the new value?

Percentage revisionincrease/decreaseMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 117
  2. B. 129.6
  3. C. 117.6
  4. D. 105.6
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 117.6

Explanation: New value = old × (100 + increase)% = 105 × 112/100 = 117.6.

Shortcut: Increase by x% means multiply by (100+x)/100.

Q2. Given array [3, 4, 9, 5], what value will a maximum-finding loop return?

Coding focusdry runMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 3
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 21
  4. D. 9
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 9

Explanation: Compare each element and keep the largest. The maximum among [3, 4, 9, 5] is 9.

Shortcut: Initialize max with first element, then compare one by one.

Q3. Classify the string 'tcs' after comparing it with its reverse.

One easy coding problemloop traceHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Not palindrome
  2. B. Anagram
  3. C. Empty string
  4. D. Sorted string
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Not palindrome

Explanation: Reverse of 'tcs' is 'sct'. Since it does not match, it is not palindrome.

Shortcut: Palindrome check: compare first with last, second with second last.

Q4. What does a factorial function return for input 9?

One medium coding problemarray/string logicEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 362889
  2. B. 362879
  3. C. 40320
  4. D. 362880
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 362880

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 9. 9! = 362880.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

Q5. What is the time complexity of a single loop that visits each element of an array once?

Programming logic MCQsoutput predictionMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. O(n²)
  2. B. O(log n)
  3. C. O(1)
  4. D. O(n)
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. O(n)

Explanation: A single loop over n elements performs work proportional to n. Therefore time complexity is O(n).

Shortcut: One full traversal usually means O(n).

Q6. What is the unit digit of 13^56?

Score improvement planunit digitMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 7
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 5
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 1

Explanation: Unit digits follow cycles. For base 13, calculate powers modulo 10. Since 56 is reduced within the unit-digit cycle, the unit digit is 1.

Shortcut: Use the unit digit cycle; never expand the full power.

Q7. Choose the word closest in meaning to 'kind and generous'.

Verbal ability revisionsentence fitHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. hostile
  2. B. benevolent
  3. C. ordinary
  4. D. careless
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. benevolent

Explanation: 'Benevolent' means kind, generous, and willing to help others.

Shortcut: Break benevolent as 'benefit-giving' in memory.

Q8. What is the value of 8 % 5 in C-style programming?

Coding focusloop traceEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 13
  3. C. 40
  4. D. 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 3

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 8 divided by 5 leaves remainder 3.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q9. Given array [3, 4, 9, 4], what value will a maximum-finding loop return?

One easy coding problemarray/string logicMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 3
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 20
  4. D. 9
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 9

Explanation: Compare each element and keep the largest. The maximum among [3, 4, 9, 4] is 9.

Shortcut: Initialize max with first element, then compare one by one.

Q10. Classify the string 'level' after comparing it with its reverse.

One medium coding problemoutput predictionMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. Palindrome
  2. B. Empty string
  3. C. Anagram
  4. D. Sorted string
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Palindrome

Explanation: Reverse of 'level' is 'level'. Since it matches, it is palindrome.

Shortcut: Palindrome check: compare first with last, second with second last.

Q11. What does a factorial function return for input 6?

Programming logic MCQsdebuggingHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 719
  2. B. 720
  3. C. 120
  4. D. 726
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 720

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 6. 6! = 720.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

Q12. Find the remainder when (7 × 56 + 27) is divided by 10.

Score improvement planremainderEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 0
  2. B. 2
  3. C. 1
  4. D. 9
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 9

Explanation: First calculate 7 × 56 + 27 = 419. Divide by 10. The remainder is 9.

Shortcut: For remainder questions, reduce each term by the divisor before multiplying.

Q13. A person travels 185 km at 45 km/h and 132 km at 40 km/h. Find the average speed.

Speed and distance revisionaverage speedMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 42.77
  2. B. 42.5
  3. C. 37.77
  4. D. 47.77
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 42.77

Explanation: Average speed = total distance / total time = (185+132) / (185/45+132/40) = 42.77 km/h.

Shortcut: Never average speeds directly unless equal time is given.

Q14. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=7; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

Coding focusarray/string logicMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 28
  2. B. 49
  3. C. 27
  4. D. 29
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 28

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 7. Sum = 7×(7+1)/2 = 28. Output is 28.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

Q15. What is the value of 5 % 5 in C-style programming?

One easy coding problemoutput predictionHardConcept Practice
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 0
  3. C. 10
  4. D. 25
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 0

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 5 divided by 5 leaves remainder 0.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q16. Given array [3, 4, 9, 3], what value will a maximum-finding loop return?

One medium coding problemdebuggingEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 9
  2. B. 3
  3. C. 4
  4. D. 19
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 9

Explanation: Compare each element and keep the largest. The maximum among [3, 4, 9, 3] is 9.

Shortcut: Initialize max with first element, then compare one by one.

Q17. Classify the string 'tcs' after comparing it with its reverse.

Programming logic MCQsdry runMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Not palindrome
  2. B. Anagram
  3. C. Empty string
  4. D. Sorted string
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Not palindrome

Explanation: Reverse of 'tcs' is 'sct'. Since it does not match, it is not palindrome.

Shortcut: Palindrome check: compare first with last, second with second last.

Q18. Using divisibility rules, which statement is true for 162?

Score improvement planprime/compositeMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. multiple of 11
  2. B. prime number
  3. C. divisible by 9
  4. D. divisible by 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. divisible by 3

Explanation: Sum of digits of 162 is 9. A number is divisible by 3 when the digit sum is divisible by 3. Therefore, 162 is divisible by 3.

Shortcut: For 3 and 9, check digit sum instead of division.

Q19. One card is drawn from a standard deck of 52 cards. What is the probability of drawing a heart?

Probability revisioncountingHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1/52
  2. B. 4/52
  3. C. 13/52
  4. D. 26/52
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 13/52

Explanation: There are 13 hearts in 52 cards. Probability = 13/52 = 1/4.

Shortcut: In a deck: 4 suits, 13 cards each.

Q20. A loop counts even numbers in [2, 3, 4, 5, 6]. What count is printed?

Coding focusoutput predictionEasyConcept Practice
  1. A. 20
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 2
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 3

Explanation: Even numbers are 2, 4 and 6, so the count is 3.

Shortcut: For count problems, increase counter only when condition is true.

Q21. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=4; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

One easy coding problemdebuggingMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 10
  2. B. 16
  3. C. 9
  4. D. 11
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 10

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 4. Sum = 4×(4+1)/2 = 10. Output is 10.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

Q22. What is the value of 7 % 5 in C-style programming?

One medium coding problemdry runMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 12
  2. B. 1
  3. C. 2
  4. D. 35
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 2

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 7 divided by 5 leaves remainder 2.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q23. Given array [3, 4, 9, 6], what value will a maximum-finding loop return?

Programming logic MCQsloop traceHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 4
  2. B. 22
  3. C. 9
  4. D. 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 9

Explanation: Compare each element and keep the largest. The maximum among [3, 4, 9, 6] is 9.

Shortcut: Initialize max with first element, then compare one by one.

Q24. Classify the number 80.

Score improvement planplace valueEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Even
  2. B. Odd only
  3. C. Co-prime only
  4. D. Composite
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Composite

Explanation: Check divisibility up to √80. Since a divisor is found, 80 is composite.

Shortcut: To test prime, check only divisors up to square root.

Q25. Find the next number in the series: 2, 8, 14, 20, 26, ?

Reasoning series revisiondifferenceMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 26
  2. B. 38
  3. C. 32
  4. D. 34
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 32

Explanation: The difference is constant: 6. Next term = 26 + 6 = 32.

Shortcut: First check constant difference.

Coding focusdebuggingMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. O(n²)
  2. B. O(log n)
  3. C. O(1)
  4. D. O(n)
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. O(n)

Explanation: A single loop over n elements performs work proportional to n. Therefore time complexity is O(n).

Shortcut: One full traversal usually means O(n).

Q27. A loop counts even numbers in [2, 3, 4, 5, 6]. What count is printed?

One easy coding problemdry runHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 20
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 2
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 3

Explanation: Even numbers are 2, 4 and 6, so the count is 3.

Shortcut: For count problems, increase counter only when condition is true.

Q28. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=6; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

One medium coding problemloop traceEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 22
  2. B. 20
  3. C. 21
  4. D. 36
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 21

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 6. Sum = 6×(6+1)/2 = 21. Output is 21.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

Q29. What is the value of 9 % 5 in C-style programming?

Programming logic MCQsarray/string logicMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 14
  2. B. 1
  3. C. 4
  4. D. 45
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 4

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 9 divided by 5 leaves remainder 4.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q30. In the number 8589, what is the place value of the digit 5?

Score improvement plandivisibilityMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 50
  2. B. 5
  3. C. 500
  4. D. 5000
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 500

Explanation: Place value means digit multiplied by its position value. In 8589, digit 5 is at the 100 place, so place value = 5 × 100 = 500.

Shortcut: Read from right: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands. Multiply the digit by its place.

Q31. A value 255 is increased by 18%. What is the new value?

Percentage revisioncomparisonHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 273
  2. B. 282.9
  3. C. 300.9
  4. D. 318.9
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 300.9

Explanation: New value = old × (100 + increase)% = 255 × 118/100 = 300.9.

Shortcut: Increase by x% means multiply by (100+x)/100.

Coding focusdry runEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 5040
  2. B. 720
  3. C. 5047
  4. D. 5039
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 5040

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 7. 7! = 5040.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

  4. D. O(n)

Q35. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=3; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

Programming logic MCQsoutput predictionHardConcept Practice
  1. A. 9
  2. B. 5
  3. C. 6
  4. D. 7

Answer: C. 6

Q36. What is the unit digit of 11^86?

Score improvement planunit digitEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 7
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 5
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 1

Explanation: Unit digits follow cycles. For base 11, calculate powers modulo 10. Since 86 is reduced within the unit-digit cycle, the unit digit is 1.

Shortcut: Use the unit digit cycle; never expand the full power.

Q39. What does a factorial function return for input 9?

One easy coding problemarray/string logicHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 362889
  2. B. 362879
  3. C. 40320
  4. D. 362880
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 362880

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 9. 9! = 362880.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

Q40. What is the time complexity of a single loop that visits each element of an array once?

One medium coding problemoutput predictionEasyConcept Practice
  1. A. O(n²)
  2. B. O(log n)
  3. C. O(1)
  4. D. O(n)
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. O(n)

Explanation: A single loop over n elements performs work proportional to n. Therefore time complexity is O(n).

Shortcut: One full traversal usually means O(n).

Q41. A loop counts even numbers in [2, 3, 4, 5, 6]. What count is printed?

Programming logic MCQsdebuggingMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 20
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 2
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 3

Explanation: Even numbers are 2, 4 and 6, so the count is 3.

Shortcut: For count problems, increase counter only when condition is true.

Q42. Find the remainder when (10 × 86 + 27) is divided by 5.

Score improvement planremainderMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 2
  2. B. 0
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 2

Explanation: First calculate 10 × 86 + 27 = 887. Divide by 5. The remainder is 2.

Shortcut: For remainder questions, reduce each term by the divisor before multiplying.

Q43. A person travels 335 km at 45 km/h and 252 km at 40 km/h. Find the average speed.

Speed and distance revisionaverage speedHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 37.71
  2. B. 42.5
  3. C. 42.71
  4. D. 47.71
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 42.71

Explanation: Average speed = total distance / total time = (335+252) / (335/45+252/40) = 42.71 km/h.

Shortcut: Never average speeds directly unless equal time is given.

Q44. Given array [3, 4, 9, 3], what value will a maximum-finding loop return?

Coding focusarray/string logicEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 9
  2. B. 3
  3. C. 4
  4. D. 19
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 9

Explanation: Compare each element and keep the largest. The maximum among [3, 4, 9, 3] is 9.

Shortcut: Initialize max with first element, then compare one by one.

Q45. Classify the string 'tcs' after comparing it with its reverse.

One easy coding problemoutput predictionMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. Not palindrome
  2. B. Anagram
  3. C. Empty string
  4. D. Sorted string
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Not palindrome

Explanation: Reverse of 'tcs' is 'sct'. Since it does not match, it is not palindrome.

Shortcut: Palindrome check: compare first with last, second with second last.

Q46. What does a factorial function return for input 6?

One medium coding problemdebuggingMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 719
  2. B. 720
  3. C. 120
  4. D. 726
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 720

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 6. 6! = 720.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

Q47. What is the time complexity of a single loop that visits each element of an array once?

Programming logic MCQsdry runHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. O(n²)
  2. B. O(log n)
  3. C. O(1)
  4. D. O(n)
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. O(n)

Explanation: A single loop over n elements performs work proportional to n. Therefore time complexity is O(n).

Shortcut: One full traversal usually means O(n).

Q48. Using divisibility rules, which statement is true for 192?

Score improvement planprime/compositeEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. multiple of 11
  2. B. prime number
  3. C. divisible by 9
  4. D. divisible by 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. divisible by 3

Explanation: Sum of digits of 192 is 12. A number is divisible by 3 when the digit sum is divisible by 3. Therefore, 192 is divisible by 3.

Shortcut: For 3 and 9, check digit sum instead of division.

Q49. One card is drawn from a standard deck of 52 cards. What is the probability of drawing a heart?

Probability revisioncountingMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1/52
  2. B. 4/52
  3. C. 13/52
  4. D. 26/52
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 13/52

Explanation: There are 13 hearts in 52 cards. Probability = 13/52 = 1/4.

Shortcut: In a deck: 4 suits, 13 cards each.

Q50. What is the value of 5 % 5 in C-style programming?

Coding focusoutput predictionMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 1
  2. B. 0
  3. C. 10
  4. D. 25
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 0

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 5 divided by 5 leaves remainder 0.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q51. Given array [3, 4, 9, 6], what value will a maximum-finding loop return?

One easy coding problemdebuggingHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 4
  2. B. 22
  3. C. 9
  4. D. 3
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 9

Explanation: Compare each element and keep the largest. The maximum among [3, 4, 9, 6] is 9.

Shortcut: Initialize max with first element, then compare one by one.

Q52. Classify the string 'level' after comparing it with its reverse.

One medium coding problemdry runEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Palindrome
  2. B. Empty string
  3. C. Anagram
  4. D. Sorted string
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Palindrome

Explanation: Reverse of 'level' is 'level'. Since it matches, it is palindrome.

Shortcut: Palindrome check: compare first with last, second with second last.

Q53. What does a factorial function return for input 8?

Programming logic MCQsloop traceMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 40328
  2. B. 40320
  3. C. 5040
  4. D. 40319
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 40320

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 8. 8! = 40320.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

Q54. Classify the number 110.

Score improvement planplace valueMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Even
  2. B. Odd only
  3. C. Co-prime only
  4. D. Composite
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Composite

Explanation: Check divisibility up to √110. Since a divisor is found, 110 is composite.

Shortcut: To test prime, check only divisors up to square root.

Q55. Find the next number in the series: 2, 8, 14, 20, 26, ?

Reasoning series revisiondifferenceHardConcept Practice
  1. A. 26
  2. B. 38
  3. C. 32
  4. D. 34
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 32

Explanation: The difference is constant: 6. Next term = 26 + 6 = 32.

Shortcut: First check constant difference.

Q56. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=4; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

Coding focusdebuggingEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 10
  2. B. 16
  3. C. 9
  4. D. 11
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 10

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 4. Sum = 4×(4+1)/2 = 10. Output is 10.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

Q57. What is the value of 7 % 5 in C-style programming?

One easy coding problemdry runMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 12
  2. B. 1
  3. C. 2
  4. D. 35
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 2

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 7 divided by 5 leaves remainder 2.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q58. Given array [3, 4, 9, 5], what value will a maximum-finding loop return?

One medium coding problemloop traceMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 3
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 21
  4. D. 9
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 9

Explanation: Compare each element and keep the largest. The maximum among [3, 4, 9, 5] is 9.

Shortcut: Initialize max with first element, then compare one by one.

Q59. Classify the string 'tcs' after comparing it with its reverse.

Programming logic MCQsarray/string logicHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Not palindrome
  2. B. Anagram
  3. C. Empty string
  4. D. Sorted string
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Not palindrome

Explanation: Reverse of 'tcs' is 'sct'. Since it does not match, it is not palindrome.

Shortcut: Palindrome check: compare first with last, second with second last.

Q60. In the number 8859, what is the place value of the digit 8?

Score improvement plandivisibilityEasyConcept Practice
  1. A. 8000
  2. B. 8
  3. C. 800
  4. D. 80
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 800

Explanation: Place value means digit multiplied by its position value. In 8859, digit 8 is at the 100 place, so place value = 8 × 100 = 800.

Shortcut: Read from right: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands. Multiply the digit by its place.

Q61. A value 405 is increased by 24%. What is the new value?

Percentage revisionsalary basedMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 429
  2. B. 502.2
  3. C. 526.2
  4. D. 478.2
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 502.2

Explanation: New value = old × (100 + increase)% = 405 × 124/100 = 502.2.

Shortcut: Increase by x% means multiply by (100+x)/100.

Q62. A loop counts even numbers in [2, 3, 4, 5, 6]. What count is printed?

Coding focusdry runMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 20
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 2
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 3

Explanation: Even numbers are 2, 4 and 6, so the count is 3.

Shortcut: For count problems, increase counter only when condition is true.

Q63. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=6; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

One easy coding problemloop traceHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 22
  2. B. 20
  3. C. 21
  4. D. 36
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 21

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 6. Sum = 6×(6+1)/2 = 21. Output is 21.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

Q64. What is the value of 9 % 5 in C-style programming?

One medium coding problemarray/string logicEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 14
  2. B. 1
  3. C. 4
  4. D. 45
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 4

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 9 divided by 5 leaves remainder 4.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q65. Given array [3, 4, 9, 4], what value will a maximum-finding loop return?

Programming logic MCQsoutput predictionMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 3
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 20
  4. D. 9
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. 9

Explanation: Compare each element and keep the largest. The maximum among [3, 4, 9, 4] is 9.

Shortcut: Initialize max with first element, then compare one by one.

End-of-Day Revision Card — Day 27

10-point summary

  1. Revise the definition and purpose of Full mock test 2.
  2. Write formulas once by hand before attempting speed questions.
  3. Solve easy questions first to build accuracy.
  4. Use option elimination wherever full calculation is long.
  5. Track every mistake in the weak-area dashboard.
  6. Reattempt wrong quiz questions without seeing the answer.
  7. Mark completed topics only after solving examples.
  8. Keep verbal practice short but consistent.
  9. Use shortcuts only after understanding the concept.
  10. End the day by reviewing tomorrow’s topic list.

Formula recap

  • Loop dry run: Track variable after each iteration — Use a small table.
  • Modulo: a % b = remainder — Useful for digit and divisibility code.
  • Array traversal: Index 0 to n-1 — Avoid out-of-bound errors.
  • Divisibility by 2: Last digit is even — Use for quick even/odd and factor checks.
  • Divisibility by 3: Sum of digits divisible by 3 — Use before doing long division.

Tricks recap

  • Full mock test 2: Before solving Full mock test 2, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • Coding focus: Before solving Coding focus, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • One easy coding problem: Before solving One easy coding problem, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • One medium coding problem: Before solving One medium coding problem, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Mistake checklist

  • Wrong base value
  • Wrong unit conversion
  • Misread option
  • Skipped condition
  • No revision of incorrect answers

Weak-area revision advice: Any topic below 60% must be revised before moving to hard questions.

Day 28 — Targeted Weak Topic Revision

Study objective: Build practical command over Targeted weak topic revision, Top 10 quant weak areas, Top 10 reasoning weak areas, Top 10 verbal weak areas and apply it in TCS NQT-style timed questions.

  • Targeted weak topic revision

  • Top 10 verbal weak areas

Targeted weak topic revision

Targeted weak topic revision is an important part of the TCS NQT preparation plan. Understand the definition, observe solved examples, then practise mixed questions.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Targeted weak topic revision.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Targeted weak topic revision, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Targeted weak topic revision; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember Concept trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Targeted weak topic revision.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Targeted weak topic revision is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Concept trick, Pattern recognition trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: Choose correct: 'He go' or 'He goes'.
'He goes' is correct because singular subject uses singular verb.
Shortcut: Check subject first.
Example 2: Synonym of rapid.
Rapid means fast or quick.
Shortcut: Use context clue.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Targeted weak topic revision without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.

Top 10 verbal weak areas

Top 10 verbal weak areas improves comprehension, grammar, and vocabulary. Read the sentence meaning first, then check grammar rules. In comprehension, answer only from the passage, not from personal knowledge.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Top 10 verbal weak areas.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.

Top coding weak areas

Top coding weak areas builds programming logic for TCS NQT coding and MCQ rounds. Focus on dry runs, variable updates, loop boundaries, array indexing, string traversal, and base conditions in recursion.

  1. Step 1: Read the question and identify that it belongs to Top coding weak areas.
  2. Step 2: Write the given values or conditions in short form.
  3. Step 3: Choose the correct rule, formula, or logic pattern.
  4. Step 4: Solve neatly and compare with options.
  5. Step 5: Recheck units, signs, and hidden conditions before final answer.

Tricks to Remember

Concept trick

Before solving Top coding weak areas, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Formula shortcut

Write only the necessary formula for Top coding weak areas; avoid using long methods when an option-based shortcut works.

Memory method

Remember Dry run trick as the first shortcut for this topic.

Pattern recognition trick

Look for repeated structures, ratios, signs, keywords, and option patterns in Top coding weak areas.

Option elimination trick

Eliminate impossible options using parity, magnitude, grammar sense, or boundary values.

Common trap

The usual trap in Top coding weak areas is using the wrong base, wrong unit, wrong order, or assuming extra information.

30-second solving method

Convert the question into one line of formula or one small diagram, then test the closest option.

Fast solving method for TCS NQT

Use Dry run trick, Boundary check trick and avoid lengthy manual expansion.

Solved Examples

Example 1: for i=1 to 3, add i to sum.
sum starts 0. After i=1 sum=1, i=2 sum=3, i=3 sum=6. Output 6.
Shortcut: Make trace table.
Example 2: Check if 'madam' is palindrome.
Reverse is 'madam', same as original, so palindrome.
Shortcut: Compare both ends.

Common Mistakes

  • Starting Top coding weak areas without identifying the exact concept.
  • Copying numbers incorrectly from the question.
  • Selecting the first matching option without checking all conditions.
  • Ignoring units, signs, order, tense, or base value.
  • Not reviewing wrong answers after practice.

Important Formulas and Rules

Formula/RuleExpressionWhen to use
Subject-verb agreementSingular subject takes singular verbCheck subject before verb.
Article usagea/an/theUse an before vowel sound.
PrepositionsDepend on verb phraseLearn common pairs.
Loop dry runTrack variable after each iterationUse a small table.
Moduloa % b = remainderUseful for digit and divisibility code.
Array traversalIndex 0 to n-1Avoid out-of-bound errors.

Practice Question Bank — 65 Questions

All questions below are original TCS NQT PYQ-style or concept-practice questions with answers and explanations.

Q1. A value 105 is increased by 12%. What is the new value?

Percentage revisionincrease/decreaseMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 117
  2. B. 129.6
  3. C. 117.6
  4. D. 105.6
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 117.6

Explanation: New value = old × (100 + increase)% = 105 × 112/100 = 117.6.

Shortcut: Increase by x% means multiply by (100+x)/100.

Q2. Marked price is ₹1050 and discount is 25%. Find the selling price.

Profit, Loss and SI revisionmarked priceMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 762.5
  2. B. 1025
  3. C. 787.5
  4. D. 812.5
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 787.5

Explanation: SP after discount = MP × (100-discount)/100 = 1050 × 75/100 = ₹787.5.

Shortcut: Discount is always calculated on marked price unless stated otherwise.

Q3. A train of length 135 m runs at 63 km/h. How much time will it take to cross a pole?

Speed and distance revisiontrain crossingHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 12.71
  2. B. 2.14
  3. C. 7.71
  4. D. 2.71
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 7.71

Explanation: Convert speed: 63 km/h = 17.5 m/s. Time = length/speed = 135/17.5 = 7.71 seconds.

Shortcut: For trains, convert km/h to m/s by multiplying by 5/18.

Q4. Para-jumble: (1) and improves accuracy. (2) Daily reading builds speed. (3) It also increases vocabulary. (4) Therefore it helps in verbal tests. Choose the best order.

Top 10 verbal weak areaserror spottingEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 3-2-1-4
  2. B. 1-2-3-4
  3. C. 2-1-4-3
  4. D. Arrange: 2-3-1-4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Arrange: 2-3-1-4

Explanation: Sentence 2 introduces daily reading. Sentence 3 adds vocabulary benefit. Sentence 1 continues another benefit. Sentence 4 concludes. Order is 2-3-1-4.

Shortcut: Find opening sentence first, then connectors like also/therefore.

Q5. What is the time complexity of a single loop that visits each element of an array once?

Top coding weak areasoutput predictionMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. O(n²)
  2. B. O(log n)
  3. C. O(1)
  4. D. O(n)
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. O(n)

Explanation: A single loop over n elements performs work proportional to n. Therefore time complexity is O(n).

Shortcut: One full traversal usually means O(n).

Q6. A person walks 5 km North, then 3 km East, then 2 km South. Where is he from the starting point?

Logical reasoning revisionshortcutMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 3 km West and 3 km North
  2. B. 6 km South
  3. C. 3 km East and 3 km North of starting point
  4. D. 3 km East and 3 km North
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 3 km East and 3 km North of starting point

Explanation: North-South net = 5-2 = 3 km North. East-West net = 3 km East. So position is 3 km East and 3 km North of starting point.

Shortcut: Draw N-E-S-W and cancel opposite directions.

Q7. Choose the word closest in meaning to 'kind and generous'.

Verbal ability revisionsentence fitHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. hostile
  2. B. benevolent
  3. C. ordinary
  4. D. careless
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. benevolent

Explanation: 'Benevolent' means kind, generous, and willing to help others.

Shortcut: Break benevolent as 'benefit-giving' in memory.

Q8. What is the value of 8 % 2 in C-style programming?

Programming logic revisionloop traceEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 16
  2. B. 0
  3. C. 10
  4. D. 4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 0

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 8 divided by 2 leaves remainder 0.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q9. One card is drawn from a standard deck of 52 cards. What is the probability of drawing a heart?

Probability revisioncountingMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1/52
  2. B. 4/52
  3. C. 13/52
  4. D. 26/52
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 13/52

Explanation: There are 13 hearts in 52 cards. Probability = 13/52 = 1/4.

Shortcut: In a deck: 4 suits, 13 cards each.

Q10. Para-jumble: (1) and improves accuracy. (2) Daily reading builds speed. (3) It also increases vocabulary. (4) Therefore it helps in verbal tests. Choose the best order.

Top 10 verbal weak areasgrammarMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 3-2-1-4
  2. B. 1-2-3-4
  3. C. 2-1-4-3
  4. D. Arrange: 2-3-1-4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Arrange: 2-3-1-4

Explanation: Sentence 2 introduces daily reading. Sentence 3 adds vocabulary benefit. Sentence 1 continues another benefit. Sentence 4 concludes. Order is 2-3-1-4.

Shortcut: Find opening sentence first, then connectors like also/therefore.

Q11. What does a factorial function return for input 6?

Top coding weak areasdebuggingHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 719
  2. B. 720
  3. C. 120
  4. D. 726
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 720

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 6. 6! = 720.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

Q12. Marked price is ₹1300 and discount is 25%. Find the selling price.

Profit, Loss and SI revisionmarked priceEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 975
  2. B. 950
  3. C. 1000
  4. D. 1275
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 975

Explanation: SP after discount = MP × (100-discount)/100 = 1300 × 75/100 = ₹975.

Shortcut: Discount is always calculated on marked price unless stated otherwise.

Q13. A person travels 185 km at 45 km/h and 132 km at 45 km/h. Find the average speed.

Speed and distance revisionaverage speedMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 45
  2. B. 40
  3. C. 50
  4. D. 53
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 45

Explanation: Average speed = total distance / total time = (185+132) / (185/45+132/45) = 45 km/h.

Shortcut: Never average speeds directly unless equal time is given.

Q14. Total work is considered 190 units. If 45 units are completed, how many units remain?

Time and work revisionwork comparisonMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 45
  2. B. 145
  3. C. 235
  4. D. 155
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 145

Explanation: Remaining work = total work - completed work = 190-45 = 145 units.

Shortcut: In LCM method, always subtract completed work from total work.

Q15. Find the next number in the series: 2, 5, 8, 11, 14, ?

Reasoning series revisiondifferenceHardConcept Practice
  1. A. 14
  2. B. 17
  3. C. 20
  4. D. 19
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 17

Explanation: The difference is constant: 3. Next term = 14 + 3 = 17.

Shortcut: First check constant difference.

Q16. Para-jumble: (1) and improves accuracy. (2) Daily reading builds speed. (3) It also increases vocabulary. (4) Therefore it helps in verbal tests. Choose the best order.

Top 10 verbal weak areasvocabularyEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 3-2-1-4
  2. B. 1-2-3-4
  3. C. 2-1-4-3
  4. D. Arrange: 2-3-1-4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Arrange: 2-3-1-4

Explanation: Sentence 2 introduces daily reading. Sentence 3 adds vocabulary benefit. Sentence 1 continues another benefit. Sentence 4 concludes. Order is 2-3-1-4.

Shortcut: Find opening sentence first, then connectors like also/therefore.

Q17. Classify the string 'tcs' after comparing it with its reverse.

Top coding weak areasdry runMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Not palindrome
  2. B. Anagram
  3. C. Empty string
  4. D. Sorted string
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Not palindrome

Explanation: Reverse of 'tcs' is 'sct'. Since it does not match, it is not palindrome.

Shortcut: Palindrome check: compare first with last, second with second last.

Q18. What does a factorial function return for input 8?

Programming logic revisionloop traceMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 40328
  2. B. 40320
  3. C. 5040
  4. D. 40319
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 40320

Explanation: Factorial means product from 1 to 8. 8! = 40320.

Shortcut: Recursive factorial: fact(n)=n×fact(n-1), base fact(0)=1.

Q19. One card is drawn from a standard deck of 52 cards. What is the probability of drawing a heart?

Probability revisioncountingHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1/52
  2. B. 4/52
  3. C. 13/52
  4. D. 26/52
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 13/52

Explanation: There are 13 hearts in 52 cards. Probability = 13/52 = 1/4.

Shortcut: In a deck: 4 suits, 13 cards each.

Q20. In the number 8536, what is the place value of the digit 5?

Number System revisiondivisibilityEasyConcept Practice
  1. A. 50
  2. B. 5
  3. C. 500
  4. D. 5000
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 500

Explanation: Place value means digit multiplied by its position value. In 8536, digit 5 is at the 100 place, so place value = 5 × 100 = 500.

Shortcut: Read from right: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands. Multiply the digit by its place.

Q21. Convert 1/3 into percentage.

Percentage revisionbasic percentageMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 38.33
  2. B. 33.33
  3. C. 300
  4. D. 28.33
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 33.33

Explanation: Percentage = fraction × 100 = 1/3 × 100 = 33.33%.

Shortcut: Remember common fractions: 1/2=50%, 1/4=25%, 1/5=20%, 1/8=12.5%.

Q22. Para-jumble: (1) and improves accuracy. (2) Daily reading builds speed. (3) It also increases vocabulary. (4) Therefore it helps in verbal tests. Choose the best order.

Top 10 verbal weak areassentence fitMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 3-2-1-4
  2. B. 1-2-3-4
  3. C. 2-1-4-3
  4. D. Arrange: 2-3-1-4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Arrange: 2-3-1-4

Explanation: Sentence 2 introduces daily reading. Sentence 3 adds vocabulary benefit. Sentence 1 continues another benefit. Sentence 4 concludes. Order is 2-3-1-4.

Shortcut: Find opening sentence first, then connectors like also/therefore.

Q23. Given array [4, 4, 9, 6], what value will a maximum-finding loop return?

Top coding weak areasloop traceHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 4
  2. B. 9
  3. C. 17
  4. D. 23
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 9

Explanation: Compare each element and keep the largest. The maximum among [4, 4, 9, 6] is 9.

Shortcut: Initialize max with first element, then compare one by one.

Q24. Total work is considered 240 units. If 45 units are completed, how many units remain?

Time and work revisionwork comparisonEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 205
  2. B. 195
  3. C. 285
  4. D. 45
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 195

Explanation: Remaining work = total work - completed work = 240-45 = 195 units.

Shortcut: In LCM method, always subtract completed work from total work.

  1. A. 14
  2. B. 17

Answer: B. 17

Q26. A person walks 7 km North, then 3 km East, then 4 km South. Where is he from the starting point?

Logical reasoning revisionshortcutMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 3 km West and 3 km North
  2. B. 6 km South
  3. C. 3 km East and 3 km North of starting point
  4. D. 3 km East and 3 km North
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 3 km East and 3 km North of starting point

Explanation: North-South net = 7-4 = 3 km North. East-West net = 3 km East. So position is 3 km East and 3 km North of starting point.

Shortcut: Draw N-E-S-W and cancel opposite directions.

Q27. Choose the correct passive voice sentence.

Verbal ability revisionsentence fitHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. The report has submit by Anil.
  2. B. The report were submitted by Anil.
  3. C. The report was submitted by Anil.
  4. D. The report submitted by Anil.
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. The report was submitted by Anil.

Explanation: Singular subject 'report' takes 'was'. Past participle of submit is 'submitted'.

Shortcut: Passive voice pattern: object + be verb + past participle.

Q28. Para-jumble: (1) and improves accuracy. (2) Daily reading builds speed. (3) It also increases vocabulary. (4) Therefore it helps in verbal tests. Choose the best order.

Explanation: Sentence 2 introduces daily reading. Sentence 3 adds vocabulary benefit. Sentence 1 continues another benefit. Sentence 4 concludes. Order is 2-3-1-4.

Shortcut: Find opening sentence first, then connectors like also/therefore.

Q29. What is the value of 9 % 2 in C-style programming?

Top coding weak areasarray/string logicMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 4
  2. B. 11
  3. C. 1
  4. D. 18
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 1

Explanation: The % operator gives remainder. 9 divided by 2 leaves remainder 1.

Shortcut: Modulo gives remainder, not quotient.

Q30. In the number 8626, what is the place value of the digit 6?

Number System revisiondivisibilityMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 600
  2. B. 6000
  3. C. 6
  4. D. 60
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 600

Explanation: Place value means digit multiplied by its position value. In 8626, digit 6 is at the 100 place, so place value = 6 × 100 = 600.

Shortcut: Read from right: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands. Multiply the digit by its place.

Q31. A value 255 is increased by 18%. What is the new value?

Percentage revisioncomparisonHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 273
  2. B. 282.9
  3. C. 300.9
  4. D. 318.9
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 300.9

Explanation: New value = old × (100 + increase)% = 255 × 118/100 = 300.9.

Shortcut: Increase by x% means multiply by (100+x)/100.

Q32. Marked price is ₹1800 and discount is 25%. Find the selling price.

Profit, Loss and SI revisionmarked priceEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1775
  2. B. 1325
  3. C. 1350
  4. D. 1375
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 1350

Explanation: SP after discount = MP × (100-discount)/100 = 1800 × 75/100 = ₹1350.

Shortcut: Discount is always calculated on marked price unless stated otherwise.

Q33. A train of length 285 m runs at 63 km/h. How much time will it take to cross a pole?

Speed and distance revisiontrain crossingMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 4.52
  2. B. 21.29
  3. C. 16.29
  4. D. 11.29
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 16.29

Explanation: Convert speed: 63 km/h = 17.5 m/s. Time = length/speed = 285/17.5 = 16.29 seconds.

Shortcut: For trains, convert km/h to m/s by multiplying by 5/18.

Q34. Para-jumble: (1) and improves accuracy. (2) Daily reading builds speed. (3) It also increases vocabulary. (4) Therefore it helps in verbal tests. Choose the best order.

Top 10 verbal weak areaserror spottingMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 3-2-1-4
  2. B. 1-2-3-4
  3. C. 2-1-4-3

Answer: D. Arrange: 2-3-1-4

Explanation: Sentence 2 introduces daily reading. Sentence 3 adds vocabulary benefit. Sentence 1 continues another benefit. Sentence 4 concludes. Order is 2-3-1-4.

Shortcut: Find opening sentence first, then connectors like also/therefore.

Q35. What is the output of this C-style loop?
int s=0; for(int j=1; j<=3; j++) s=s+j; printf("%d",s);

Top coding weak areasoutput predictionHardConcept Practice
  1. A. 9
  2. B. 5
  3. C. 6
  4. D. 7
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 6

Explanation: The loop adds numbers 1 to 3. Sum = 3×(3+1)/2 = 6. Output is 6.

Shortcut: Dry run small loop by tracking variable changes row-wise.

Q36. A person walks 5 km North, then 3 km East, then 2 km South. Where is he from the starting point?

Logical reasoning revisionconceptEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 3 km West and 3 km North
  2. B. 6 km South
  3. C. 3 km East and 3 km North of starting point
  4. D. 3 km East and 3 km North
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 3 km East and 3 km North of starting point

Explanation: North-South net = 5-2 = 3 km North. East-West net = 3 km East. So position is 3 km East and 3 km North of starting point.

Shortcut: Draw N-E-S-W and cancel opposite directions.

Q41. A loop counts even numbers in [2, 3, 4, 5, 6]. What count is printed?

  1. A. 20
  2. B. 4
  3. C. 3
  4. D. 2
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 3

Explanation: Even numbers are 2, 4 and 6, so the count is 3.

Shortcut: For count problems, increase counter only when condition is true.

  1. A. 1537.5
  2. B. 1512.5
  3. C. 2025
  4. D. 1562.5
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 1537.5

Explanation: SP after discount = MP × (100-discount)/100 = 2050 × 75/100 = ₹1537.5.

Shortcut: Discount is always calculated on marked price unless stated otherwise.

Q43. A person travels 335 km at 45 km/h and 252 km at 45 km/h. Find the average speed.

  1. A. 45
  2. B. 40
  3. C. 50
  4. D. 53
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 45

Explanation: Average speed = total distance / total time = (335+252) / (335/45+252/45) = 45 km/h.

Shortcut: Never average speeds directly unless equal time is given.

Q57. Choose the correct passive voice sentence.

Verbal ability revisionsentence fitMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. The report has submit by Anil.
  2. B. The report were submitted by Anil.
  3. C. The report was submitted by Anil.
  4. D. The report submitted by Anil.
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. The report was submitted by Anil.

Explanation: Singular subject 'report' takes 'was'. Past participle of submit is 'submitted'.

Shortcut: Passive voice pattern: object + be verb + past participle.

Q58. Para-jumble: (1) and improves accuracy. (2) Daily reading builds speed. (3) It also increases vocabulary. (4) Therefore it helps in verbal tests. Choose the best order.

Top 10 verbal weak areasreading inferenceMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 3-2-1-4
  2. B. 1-2-3-4
  3. C. 2-1-4-3
  4. D. Arrange: 2-3-1-4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Arrange: 2-3-1-4

Explanation: Sentence 2 introduces daily reading. Sentence 3 adds vocabulary benefit. Sentence 1 continues another benefit. Sentence 4 concludes. Order is 2-3-1-4.

Shortcut: Find opening sentence first, then connectors like also/therefore.

Q59. Classify the string 'tcs' after comparing it with its reverse.

Top coding weak areasarray/string logicHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. Not palindrome
  2. B. Anagram
  3. C. Empty string
  4. D. Sorted string
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. Not palindrome

Explanation: Reverse of 'tcs' is 'sct'. Since it does not match, it is not palindrome.

Shortcut: Palindrome check: compare first with last, second with second last.

Q60. In the number 8896, what is the place value of the digit 8?

Number System revisiondivisibilityEasyConcept Practice
  1. A. 8000
  2. B. 8
  3. C. 800
  4. D. 80
Show answer and explanation

Answer: C. 800

Explanation: Place value means digit multiplied by its position value. In 8896, digit 8 is at the 100 place, so place value = 8 × 100 = 800.

Shortcut: Read from right: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands. Multiply the digit by its place.

Q61. A value 405 is increased by 24%. What is the new value?

Percentage revisionsalary basedMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 429
  2. B. 502.2
  3. C. 526.2
  4. D. 478.2
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 502.2

Explanation: New value = old × (100 + increase)% = 405 × 124/100 = 502.2.

Shortcut: Increase by x% means multiply by (100+x)/100.

Q62. Marked price is ₹2550 and discount is 25%. Find the selling price.

Profit, Loss and SI revisionmarked priceMediumTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 1937.5
  2. B. 1912.5
  3. C. 2525
  4. D. 1887.5
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 1912.5

Explanation: SP after discount = MP × (100-discount)/100 = 2550 × 75/100 = ₹1912.5.

Shortcut: Discount is always calculated on marked price unless stated otherwise.

Q63. A train of length 435 m runs at 63 km/h. How much time will it take to cross a pole?

Speed and distance revisiontrain crossingHardTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 24.86
  2. B. 6.9
  3. C. 29.86
  4. D. 19.86
Show answer and explanation

Answer: A. 24.86

Explanation: Convert speed: 63 km/h = 17.5 m/s. Time = length/speed = 435/17.5 = 24.86 seconds.

Shortcut: For trains, convert km/h to m/s by multiplying by 5/18.

Q64. Para-jumble: (1) and improves accuracy. (2) Daily reading builds speed. (3) It also increases vocabulary. (4) Therefore it helps in verbal tests. Choose the best order.

Top 10 verbal weak areaserror spottingEasyTCS NQT PYQ-style
  1. A. 3-2-1-4
  2. B. 1-2-3-4
  3. C. 2-1-4-3
  4. D. Arrange: 2-3-1-4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: D. Arrange: 2-3-1-4

Explanation: Sentence 2 introduces daily reading. Sentence 3 adds vocabulary benefit. Sentence 1 continues another benefit. Sentence 4 concludes. Order is 2-3-1-4.

Shortcut: Find opening sentence first, then connectors like also/therefore.

Q65. Given array [4, 4, 9, 4], what value will a maximum-finding loop return?

Top coding weak areasoutput predictionMediumConcept Practice
  1. A. 21
  2. B. 9
  3. C. 17
  4. D. 4
Show answer and explanation

Answer: B. 9

Explanation: Compare each element and keep the largest. The maximum among [4, 4, 9, 4] is 9.

Shortcut: Initialize max with first element, then compare one by one.

End-of-Day Revision Card — Day 28

10-point summary

  1. Revise the definition and purpose of Targeted weak topic revision.
  2. Write formulas once by hand before attempting speed questions.
  3. Solve easy questions first to build accuracy.
  4. Use option elimination wherever full calculation is long.
  5. Track every mistake in the weak-area dashboard.
  6. Reattempt wrong quiz questions without seeing the answer.
  7. Mark completed topics only after solving examples.
  8. Keep verbal practice short but consistent.
  9. Use shortcuts only after understanding the concept.
  10. End the day by reviewing tomorrow’s topic list.

Formula recap

  • Subject-verb agreement: Singular subject takes singular verb — Check subject before verb.
  • Article usage: a/an/the — Use an before vowel sound.
  • Prepositions: Depend on verb phrase — Learn common pairs.
  • Loop dry run: Track variable after each iteration — Use a small table.
  • Modulo: a % b = remainder — Useful for digit and divisibility code.

Tricks recap

  • Targeted weak topic revision: Before solving Targeted weak topic revision, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • Top 10 quant weak areas: Before solving Top 10 quant weak areas, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • Top 10 reasoning weak areas: Before solving Top 10 reasoning weak areas, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.
  • Top 10 verbal weak areas: Before solving Top 10 verbal weak areas, identify the exact concept from the wording; the first correct identification saves most time.

Mistake checklist

  • Wrong base value
  • Wrong unit conversion
  • Misread option
  • Skipped condition
  • No revision of incorrect answers

Weak-area revision advice: Any topic below 60% must be revised before moving to hard questions.

Day 29 — TCS NQT PYQ-Style Marathon

Study objective: Build practical command over TCS NQT PYQ marathon, PYQ-style aptitude practice, PYQ-style reasoning practice, PYQ-style verbal practice and apply it in TCS NQT-style timed questions.
